items.title: Cameroun : la vacance à la présidence de la République divise le régime et l’opposition
items.description: © DR Hommes et femmes bien connus de l’opposition camerounaise demandent au Conseil constitutionnel de constater la vacance à la présidence de la République en raison d’une absence prolongée du chef de l’Etat. Le secrétaire à la communication du parti au pouvoir rejette la thèse de la vacance et envoie tout le monde au travail. 44 jours après le départ du président Paul Biya accompagné de son épouse Chantal Biya pour un ‘’court séjour privé’’ en Europe, le débat s’intensifie sur la vacance à la tête de l’État. Il oppose des personnalités politiques appartenant à des partis d’opposition à celles du sérail. Les premières, vu le temps que met le chef de l’État hors du pays, il y a lieu de constater la vacance à la présidence. ‘’Le Conseil constitutionnel doit constater la vacance au sommet de l’État’’, a lancé récemment le député Jean-Michel Nintcheu. Sans toutefois donner par la même occasion les raisons qui fondent sa position. Cette même semaine, une tribune du Pr jean Calvin Aba’a Oyono, universitaire et opposant au régime, relance le débat sur la question. Son analyse va plus loin que la constatation de la vacance. Elle touche aussi l’ambiguïté de la Constitution au sujet de cette vacance et du rôle du Conseil constitutionnel. Claire pour ce qui est du décès et de la démission, la loi fondamentale est muette pour ce qui est de la durée du chef de l’État à l’étranger pour déclencher le processus de vacance. Une situation qui paralyse le sommet de l’État, bloque les décrets urgents et la nomination d’un vice-président. Le juriste appelle à une réflexion sur le rôle du Conseil constitutionnel face à l’absence prolongée du président de la République. Fort de ces sorties en série, le secrétaire à la communication du Rassemblement démocratique du peuple camerounais-Rdpc-, parti au pouvoir, rompt le silence mardi. Il est catégorique. ‘’Il n’y a aucune vacance au sommet de l’État’’. Le ministre d’État rappelle que ‘’aucune disposition légale ou règlementaire ne fixe la durée maximale du séjour présidentiel hors du Cameroun’’. Aussi, à propos de l’immobilisme au sommet de l’État, le Pr jacques Fame Ndongo affirme que ‘’le chef de l’exécutif est au travail où qu’il se trouve’’. Par conséquent ‘’halte aux affabulations et aux gros mensonges’’, insiste-t-il. Pour lui, vacance à la présidence de la République, paralysie dans la gouvernance, il n’y a rien à voir. Il n’y a rien à entendre non plus. Il faut circuler. ‘’Que chacun se mette résolument au travail dans son domaine de compétence’’, invite le ministre de l’Enseignement supérieur. Sa sortie en date du 21 juillet ne suffit pas pour faire taire l’opposition. Une fois publiée, elle a suscité la réaction du premier vice-président du Mouvement pour la renaissance du Cameroun. Mamadou Mota dénonce la convocation des philosophes pour ‘’camoufler un vide institutionnel criant’’. Selon l’opposant, prétendre qu’aucune disposition légale ne fixe la durée maximale d’un séjour présidentiel à l’étranger pour balayer le débat relève du sophisme pur’’. 44 jours à l’étranger pour le président n’est plus un séjour, mais ‘’un exil de fait ’’ d’après lui. Et le travail du président de la République ne peut s’exercer qu’au ‘’cœur de la nation’’ et non par ‘’télétravail clandestin’’. Pour la deuxième personnalité du MRC, le chef de l’État ne peut assurer le fonctionnement régulier des pouvoirs publics en signant depuis Genève pour qu’on marque à Yaoundé. Le débat se poursuit tant que le président Paul Biya est à l’étranger. Même après son retour, les zones d’ombre qui entourent l’encadrement de la durée du président à l’étranger nécessitent un éclaircissement. A l’heure des réformes institutionnelles, le législateur gagnerait à s’y pencher pour mettre définitivement in au débat. Cet article Cameroun : la vacance à la présidence de la République divise le régime et l’opposition est apparu en premier sur Journalducameroun.com .

Nouvelle escalade des États-Unis contre l'Iran : 500 soldats américains blessés depuis la reprise du conflit (VIDÉOS)
items.description: Les etats-Unis ont mene une nouvelle serie de frappes contre des cibles militaires iraniennes, marquant la onzieme nuit consecutive d'operations. Une alerte aerienne a ete declenchee a Teheran tandis que le president americain Donald Trump a affirme que Washington n'en avait « pas fini » avec cette guerre, laissant entendre que de nouvelles attaques pourraient viser prochainement le complexe nucleaire souterrain du site de Kuh-e Kolang Gaz La, dit « Mont de la Pioche », dans le centre de l'Iran. ☫🇺🇸🇯🇴Dans le cadre de la 22e phase de l'opération Saegheh, l'Armée a ciblé des « bâtiments d’hébergement et de bien-être » et un « dépôt d’équipement » appartenant à l’armée américaine sur la base aérienne d’Al-Azraq en Jordanie pic.twitter.com/vF0Zc8Cqmd — Press TV Français (@fr_presstv) July 22, 2026 Selon le Commandement americain pour le Moyen-Orient (Centcom), ces frappes visent a reduire les capacites militaires iraniennes susceptibles de menacer la navigation dans le detroit d'Ormuz. Ce passage maritime est strategique, puisqu'il concentre une part essentielle des exportations mondiales de petrole et de gaz. Les tensions autour de cette voie commerciale ont ravive les affrontements entre Washington et Teheran debut juillet, mettant fin au protocole d'accord conclu en juin dans l'espoir d'un reglement durable. Un conflit qui s'enlise Les combats s'inscrivent dans la continuite du conflit declenche le 28 fevrier par l'offensive israelo-americaine contre l'Iran, avant un cessez-le-feu intervenu en avril. Depuis la reprise des hostilites, les affrontements se sont intensifies sur plusieurs fronts. Selon l'Associated Press, plus de 500 militaires americains ont ete blesses au cours des operations contre l'Iran. Parallelement, l'agence iranienne Fars a rapporte que des drones iraniens avaient vise des installations americaines dans plusieurs pays. L'armee iranienne affirme egalement avoir frappe des depots de munitions et des infrastructures logistiques relevant du commandement des forces terrestres americaines sur une base situee dans l'ouest du Koweït. ‼️🇧🇭 Les frappes 🇮🇷 contre les infrastructures militaires 🇺🇸 à Bahreïn se poursuivent 💥 @boris_rozhin pic.twitter.com/4dCnnWOl1u — Octar Ruga (@OctarRuga) July 21, 2026 De leur cote, les forces americaines indiquent avoir pris pour cible plusieurs infrastructures militaires iraniennes, notamment des postes de commandement, des installations navales, des hangars abritant des aeronefs, des depots de drones ainsi que des centres logistiques. Alors que les declarations de Donald Trump laissent presager une poursuite des operations, la perspective d'une nouvelle escalade militaire alimente les inquietudes quant aux consequences regionales et au coût humain et financier croissant de ce conflit.

items.title: Daily Insight for CEOs: Ecosystem leadership in a connected economy
items.description: Insight. Organizations increasingly operate within ecosystems of customers, suppliers, partners, regulators, technology providers, and communities. CEOs must think beyond organizational boundaries and understand how value is created across interconnected networks. Ecosystem leadership requires influence, collaboration, and a broader strategic perspective. Key Strategies: 1. Map key ecosystem stakeholders. 2. Strengthen strategic relationships. 3. Identify opportunities for collective value creation. 4. Foster trust and collaboration. 5. Monitor ecosystem dynamics. CEO Leadership Actions. Evaluate your organization’s ecosystem position. Strengthen engagement with key stakeholders. Promote long-term ecosystem thinking. Actionable Tip. * Identify one stakeholder relationship that could significantly enhance organizational value. Why This Matters? Strong ecosystem leadership creates resilience, influence, and sustainable growth. About the Author Ernest De-Graft Egyir, CEO advisor, Thought Leader and Founding CEO of Chief Executives Network Ghana, convenes the Ghana CEO Summit and served on Ghana’s Economic Dialogue Planning Committee.

items.title: US hits Iran, says war cost at $37.5 bn
items.description: Fresh US strikes triggered air defences in Tehran on Wednesday after President Donald Trump warned he was “not finished” with a war with Iran that has already cost his nation $37.5 billion. After the foes’ fighting over the strategic Strait of Hormuz led to the collapse of a preliminary deal, the Middle East war has expanded to include the region’s other key chokepoint for global oil supplies. Still, top US diplomat Marco Rubio reiterated Washington’s statement that it is willing to use diplomacy to find an agreement with Tehran. “The problem we are currently facing is that they are not taking the negotiations seriously,” Rubio said at a Southeast Asia ministers’ meeting in Manila. Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei has said that diplomatic exchanges with Washington through mediators were continuing. The latest round of Middle East attacks included US forces hitting targets such as “military logistics infrastructure”, while Iranian state media reported strikes around the country, including Bushehr, which hosts a nuclear power plant . “News sources reported minutes ago that air defence activity was heard in the west, east and northeast of Tehran,” state TV posted on Telegram. As the war nears the five-month mark and November legislative elections approach, Trump has faced intense political pressure to end the conflict, which is unpopular even with segments of his base. Critics have pointed to the cost of the war that Pentagon Chief Pete Hegseth said Tuesday had soared to $37.5 billion, defending a request for tens of billions in additional funding for the military. The US military said Tuesday it had launched an eleventh consecutive night of strikes against Iran, saying it targeted military sites to “further degrade Iran’s ability to threaten commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Trump had said earlier that the next US target could be an underground complex known as Pickaxe Mountain, a subterranean nuclear site near Natanz where Western intelligence suspects Iran is building an undeclared enrichment facility. Iran threatened reprisals, with the Khatam Al-Anbiya military command saying it would “consider it as an expansion of war in the region” if such an attack were carried out, threatening strikes on “all the interests of America, the allies and supporters”, state-run broadcaster IRIB reported. The warning came as Tehran broadened its offensive against US-allied Gulf states after its Houthi allies threatened to blockade Saudi ports. Two weeks after fighting resumed between the decades-old foes, Trump said Iran would need more than 20 years to recover from the damage inflicted in the Middle East war. “If we left right now, it would take Iran 20, 25 years to rebuild,” he said. “We’re not finished at all… we’re not leaving right now. – Houthi threat – Kuwait’s army said Wednesday its air defences were engaging with drone attacks from Iran, the latest round of Tehran’s reprisals. US ally Bahrain said Tuesday it intercepted Iranian attacks as sirens sounded repeatedly, while Kuwait and Jordan said they had intercepted drone and missile strikes. The Iranian army had said it targeted US assets in Kuwait and Bahrain, including air defence systems, radar installations and administrative buildings. “With this radar sweep, the enemy should prepare for even more decisive and powerful waves of drone and missile attacks,” the Guards said in a statement carried by state news agency IRNA. Trump said he would “take care of” Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels if they followed through on a threat Monday to blockade Saudi Arabia’s ports. It remains unclear how the Houthis would enforce such a blockade. But maritime intelligence provider Vanguard Tech said a Singapore-flagged tanker and a Liberia-flagged tanker both “reversed course” in the Red Sea after loading crude at the Saudi port of Yanbu. If the Houthis make good on the threat, they could endanger Riyadh’s ability to bypass the Strait of Hormuz for some oil exports, with major repercussions for the global economy. Saudi Arabia and the Houthis traded fire last week for the first time in years, threatening a 2022 truce, but the rebels have largely remained on the sidelines since Israel and the United States attacked Iran in late February. The Revolutionary Guards said Tuesday that two tankers attempting to transit the strait “were hit by explosions, resulting in massive fires that brought them to a standstill”. The UK Maritime Trade Operations agency earlier reported that an “unknown projectile” struck a tanker off the coast of Oman. The post US hits Iran, says war cost at $37.5 bn appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Poème | Nostalgies
items.description: Il arrive à l’auteur, économiste de formation et consultant international de profession, d’écrire des poèmes d’ordre métaphysique ou philosophique comme celui-ci, qui semble triste et funèbre, puisqu’il y évoque sa propre mort, mais en réalité c’est un vibrant hommage à cette merveilleuse et belle planète qu’on appelle «La Terre» ! Ce poème est un véritable hymne à la vie. Sadok Zerelli Un jour, il me faudra bien partir, Fermer les yeux sans les rouvrir, Et laisser derrière moi Cette belle planète que je n’oublierai pas * Je ne crains pas tant le silence Que l'instant où finira la danse, Quand mes yeux, pour la dernière fois, Se fermeront sur ce monde-là * Je regretterai moins de mourir Que de ne plus pouvoir sentir Le parfum d’une fleur après la pluie, Le coucher du soleil à la fin du jour, * Là où je serais J’aurai la nostalgie des matins, Du chant des oiseaux dans les jardins, Du vent léger sur les oliviers, Et du parfum du jasmin d’été. * J’aurai la nostalgie de la mer, De son horizon grand ouvert, Des vagues qui viennent mourir Comme mes rêves, sans bruit, sans soupir. * J’aurai la nostalgie des nuits étoilées, De la lune sur les toits posée, Du rire d’un enfant heureux, D’une conversation avec un ami précieux. * Je laisserai les montagnes fières, Les déserts, les forêts, les rivières, Tout ce que mes yeux ont aimé, Tout ce qui m’a émerveillé. * Certes, je n’ai pas tout compris Des mystères de la vie, Mais j’ai reconnu La douceur des mains tendues * Par quel mystère ai-je reçu Le privilège d’avoir vécu ? D’avoir connu l’aube et le soir, L’amour, la peine et l’espoir. * Pourtant, je partirai reconnaissant, D’avoir vécu, ne serait-ce qu’un instant, Sur cette merveille de l’univers Que nous appelons simplement la Terre. * Ce n’est qu’un grain de sable Perdu dans un cosmos insondable. Et pourtant, c’est sur ce petit rivage Que j’ai écrit ma courte page Et si mon corps disparaitra, mon âme demeurera, * Au-delà des siècles, au-delà du temps, Je garderai comme un dernier trésor L'amour de la vie sur cette Terre Encore et encore… NB : L’auteur s’est découvert par hasard « l’âme d’un poète», assis un soir d’été de pleine lune sur la tersasse d’un café surplombant la mer à Béni- Khiar, en relevant l’analogie qu’il y a entre la vie d’une vague, qui naît d’un frémissement de l’air, grossit, pavoise avec ses belles écumes blanches et finit par venir mourir sur la plage et se retirer sans qu’il reste aucune trace de son existence, et la vie d’un homme, qui naît du frémissement d’un spermatozoïde, grandit, pavoise avec les richesses et le pouvoir qu’il a accumulés durant sa vie et finit aussi par mourir sans laisser aucune trace non plus, car la maison qu’il aura construite sera un jour habitée par d’autres ou peut-être même rasée pour faire la place à un autre immeuble, la belle voiture qu’il conduit et dont il est si fier finira un jour à la ferraille et même ses enfants et petits-enfants finiront un jour par mourir. L’article Poème | Nostalgies est apparu en premier sur Kapitalis .

items.title: Police arrest two at Asawase Market over suspected narcotic drugs concealed in fertiliser sacks
items.description: The Manhyia District Police Command has arrested two men in connection with the unlawful possession and transportation of substances suspected to be narcotic drugs following an intelligence-led operation at the Asawase Market in Kumasi. The operation, conducted on July 21, 2026, resulted in the seizure of 100 compressed parcels of dried leaves suspected to be narcotic drugs concealed in fertiliser sacks hidden among second-hand clothing. The suspects have been identified as Osman Bassein, 37, and Mohammed Abdul-Razak, 38. In a statement signed by the Head of the Public Affairs Unit of the Ashanti Regional Police Command, Superintendent Godwin Ahianyo, the Police said the operation was launched after receiving credible intelligence that suspected narcotic drugs were being transported in fertiliser sacks. Acting on the information, a team led by the Manhyia District Commander, supported by personnel from the Manhyia Divisional Criminal Investigations Department (CID) and the Zongo Police Station, proceeded to the location and arrested the two suspects. During a search conducted in the presence of independent witnesses, officers recovered 13 fertiliser sacks. Police said further inspection revealed that the sacks contained second-hand clothing concealing 100 compressed parcels of dried leaves suspected to be narcotic drugs. The exhibits were photographed at the scene in accordance with evidential procedures and have since been secured for forensic examination. The suspects are currently in police custody assisting with investigations and will be processed in accordance with the law, subject to the outcome of the investigations. The Ashanti Regional Police Command commended members of the public for providing timely and credible information that contributed to the operation. It also encouraged the public to continue supporting law enforcement efforts by reporting suspicious activities through the Police emergency numbers 18555 (MTN toll-free), 191 or 112. Police noted that investigations are ongoing and further updates will be provided as the case progresses.

items.title: An Open Letter To The Executive Governor Of Enugu State, Dr. Peter Mbah
items.description: Your Excellency, I wish to sincerely appreciate you once again for the remarkable developmental strides that are evident across Enugu State, including my beloved community of Ette. We are particularly grateful for your recent approval of the Government Technical College (GTC), the proposed site of which is located in Ette. May the Almighty God continue to grant you the wisdom, strength, and grace to positively impact lives across every part of Enugu State. Indeed, as your administration’s slogan says, our tomorrow is here. However, I write with a deep sense of concern over an unfortunate incident that occurred on 20th July 2026 during the official handover ceremony of the proposed GTC site. This incident deserves your urgent attention to prevent unnecessary tension and preserve the peace that has long existed among our people. The proposed site for the Government Technical College is located at Ogurugu, Ette, in Igbo-Eze North Local Government Area of Enugu State. Regrettably, the Local Government Chairman of Igbo-Eze North, Hon. Michael Uchenna Ogalla, accompanied by some of his associates and other stakeholders, visited the site without officially informing or paying the traditional courtesy of notifying our revered Traditional Ruler, His Royal Highness, Innocent Alachi (Adogo Idu II). More disturbing was the deliberate renaming of the proposed site from its known and historically recognized name, Ogurugu, to Mbanabo, a name that has never existed in the history of Ette. This action has created the unfortunate impression of an attempt to alter the identity of the land and associate it with the Umuopu community, thereby laying the foundation for avoidable disputes. While we wholeheartedly welcome and appreciate the establishment of the Government Technical College in our community, we equally cherish and will continue to defend our identity, history, culture, heritage, and ancestral landmarks. No development, regardless of its significance, should come at the expense of a people’s identity and historical heritage. Your Excellency, I respectfully appeal to you to use your good office to call Hon. Michael Ogalla and all concerned parties to order and ensure that no action capable of causing confusion, misunderstanding, or conflict between the people of Ette and Umuopu is allowed to stand. The proposed GTC site should be officially recognized and referred to by its rightful and historical name, Ogurugu, Ette, and not “Mbanabo” or “Umuopu.” The peace-loving people of Ette have unwavering confidence in your commitment to justice, fairness, and equity. We trust that your administration will ensure that the truth prevails and that the identity and heritage of our community are protected. Thank you for your attention to this important matter. We pray that God Almighty will continue to bless you, strengthen you, and grant you greater success in your service to the people of Enugu State. NB: Attached is the link to the video of the official handover ceremony of the land for the proposed site. The video also shows our traditional ruler and members of the community expressing their dissatisfaction with the development – https://www.facebook.com/share/v/1CZXNqfAN8/ Amb. Dr. Honest Ameh President, Association of Indigenous People of Ette (AIPOE) Chairman, Ette Unity Forum AN OPEN LETTER TO THE EXECUTIVE GOVERNOR OF ENUGU STATE Download The post An Open Letter To The Executive Governor Of Enugu State, Dr. Peter Mbah appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Accra to host Pan-African conference on economic restructuring, political reforms
items.description: Accra is set to host more than 200 delegates from across Africa for an international conference on economic restructuring, political reforms and regional integration. The conference, scheduled for June 24, 2026, at the GNAT Hall, will bring together policymakers, academics, business leaders, traditional authorities, civil society organisations, youth leaders and development partners. Organised by Harakati Za Muungano, a pan-African movement whose name means “Union Movement” in Swahili, the conference will focus on practical strategies to accelerate Africa’s development through deeper regional integration. Key issues expected to be discussed include constitutional reforms, economic restructuring, regional market integration, industrialisation, governance continuity and sustainable financing models for development. The organisers say the discussions are expected to produce practical recommendations to inform policy decisions at both national and regional levels. Harakati Za Muungano, established in November 2023, has expanded its operations to 17 African countries, where it engages governments, regional institutions, traditional leaders, professionals, youth groups and community organisations. Its work focuses on policy advocacy, research, civic engagement and humanitarian initiatives aimed at promoting inclusive development, strengthening regional cooperation and supporting reforms to improve the quality of life of Africans. The movement advocates a more integrated Sub-Saharan Africa, where barriers to trade and movement are reduced, economic cooperation is strengthened and governments adopt policies that promote long-term planning, investment and shared prosperity. According to the organisers, greater integration could create new opportunities for businesses, boost job creation, promote industrial growth and enhance Africa’s competitiveness in the global economy. They say the conference will provide an opportunity for African leaders and stakeholders to examine bold and innovative approaches to the continent’s future while strengthening partnerships and advancing a shared vision of an economically resilient, politically stable and fully integrated Africa.

items.title: Pakistan ready to extend cooperation with Iran in all fields, Naqvi tells Iranian counterpart
items.description: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi said that Pakistan was ready to extend cooperation with Iran in all fields. He made the remarks as he met with Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni, who is currently in Pakistan on an official visit. A smartly turned-out contingent of the Federal Constabulary presented a guard of honour to the visiting minister upon his arrival, according to a statement issued by the interior ministry. It said that the two ministers held one-on-one talks, followed by delegation-level discussions. It said that the two ministers reviewed bilateral relations, trade, regional developments and avenues for cooperation. According to the interior ministry, both sides agreed to enhance cooperation in counter-terrorism, counter-narcotics, prevention of illegal immigration and efforts against human trafficking. The two ministers also emphasised the need for joint efforts to curb drug smuggling and terrorism “originating from the Afghan border”, while agreeing to improve coordination between the relevant institutions of both countries. “Pakistan is ready to increase cooperation with Iran in all fields,” Naqvi was quoted as saying. He also praised the role of Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Momeni, saying they had made “extraordinary efforts for peace”. “I hope good news will come soon and peace will be established,” he added. Momeni had arrived in Pakistan on an official visit on Monday night. On Tuesday, he met Chief of Defence Forces and Chief of the Army Staff Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir at General Headquarters in Rawalpindi, where matters of mutual interest, regional and bilateral security, and effective border management were discussed, according to Inter-Services Public Relations. He also met Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif in Islamabad, where the premier expressed concern over the escalation in the Middle East and reiterated Pakistan’s willingness to play its role as an “honest and sincere mediator and facilitator” to promote regional peace. Momeni’s visit comes as mediator Pakistan continues its diplomatic efforts to rescue the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) signed last month between the US and Iran, intended to end the conflict between the two sides that began with US-Israeli attacks on Iran in late February.

items.title: FIA says Nadra officials among 4 arrested for allegedly producing fake documents for Afghan nationals
items.description: The Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) said on Wednesday that four suspects, including officials of the National Database and Registration Authority (Nadra), had been arrested in Karachi for their alleged involvement in making false documents for Afghan nationals. In a press release, a spokesperson for the agency identified the suspects as Nadra Assistant Director Imamul Haq, Nadra Deputy Assistant Director Fayyaz Ahmed, Junaid Ahmed, a watchman working for the authority, and a private agent Muhammad Umar Shah. He added that law enforcement agencies had seized important records and other evidence as part of the operation, adding that the scope of the investigation had been expanded. “Further significant revelations and arrests are expected during the investigation, and all facilitators and key figures linked to this network will be brought to justice,” he said. The spokesperson said that preliminary investigations showed that the suspects were involved in facilitating the issuance of fake identity documents for Afghan citizens, altering records and other illegal activities. “Initial evidence points to links between this network and terrorist elements, a matter that is currently under further investigation,” the FIA spokesperson said. The FIA’s statement comes a day after the family of one of the suspects, Fayyaz Ahmed, approached police with an application for his recovery after he was reported missing. The Human Rights Commission of Pakistan had also expressed concern, demanding that the Sindh government expedite its efforts to locate the missing Nadra official. Millions of Afghans have poured into Pakistan over the past several decades, fleeing successive wars, as well as hundreds of thousands who arrived after the return of the Taliban government in 2021. In June, Pakistan had issued a fresh order for the immediate arrest of Afghan nationals residing illegally in Pakistan. Since then, the repatriation of Afghan nationals has been gaining momentum . Earlier this month, the FIA had arrested three Nadra officials in Chakwal on charges of issuing Computerised National Identity Card to an Afghan national who is in jail. In April, it had also arrested a Nadra official for his alleged “involvement” in issuing fake identity cards to Afghan nationals in Karachi.

items.title: Mondial 2026 : Sepp Blatter attaque Infantino, des critiques qui ne manquent pas d’ironie
items.description: Sepp Blatter estime que la Coupe du monde 2026 a « perdu sa crédibilité » et appelle à un changement de direction à la tête de la FIFA, visant directement la gouvernance de Gianni Infantino. Une sortie particulièrement ironique venant de l’ancien patron du football mondial, dont les 17 années à la tête de l’instance se sont achevées en pleine crise, sur fond de scandales de corruption et de graves controverses ayant durablement terni l’image de la FIFA. Deux jours après le sacre de l’Espagne face à l’Argentine (1-0) en finale, l’ancien président de la FIFA a dressé un bilan particulièrement sévère de la compétition. « La plus longue Coupe du monde s’est terminée, avec de beaux champions. Mais sur le chemin du coup de sifflet final, le tournoi a perdu sa crédibilité » , a écrit le Suisse sur X. Tout au long du Mondial 2026, Blatter avait multiplié les critiques sur l’organisation de la compétition, s’exprimant notamment sur certaines décisions de la FIFA, les contraintes entourant le tournoi ou encore les questions politiques ayant accompagné son déroulement. L’ancien dirigeant de 90 ans est allé plus loin en appelant ouvertement à une nouvelle gouvernance du football mondial. « La politique ne doit pas être autorisée à éclipser le jeu. Maintenant, c’est aux fans, aux joueurs et aux fédérations nationales de reprendre le football et de le ramener à ses racines sous une nouvelle direction » , a-t-il déclaré. Le discours prend toutefois une dimension singulière lorsqu’on se penche sur le passé de celui qui le prononce. Sepp Blatter a dirigé la FIFA de 1998 à 2015, avant que son règne ne s’achève au cœur d’une crise institutionnelle sans précédent. Son départ est intervenu dans un contexte marqué par de vastes enquêtes internationales sur des soupçons de corruption touchant de nombreux responsables du football mondial. Blatter lui-même a été suspendu par les instances disciplinaires de la FIFA et impliqué dans une longue procédure judiciaire concernant notamment un paiement de deux millions de francs suisses effectué en faveur de Michel Platini. Les deux hommes ont finalement été acquittés par la justice suisse, une issue qui doit être rappelée lorsqu’on évoque cette affaire. Gianni Infantino avait justement accédé à la présidence de la FIFA en 2016 dans un contexte de profonde crise de confiance, avec la promesse de restaurer l’image et la gouvernance de l’institution après l’ère Blatter. Les critiques de l’ancien président sur le Mondial 2026 peuvent naturellement faire l’objet d’un débat sur le fond. Mais lorsqu’il se pose en défenseur de la crédibilité et de la bonne gouvernance du football mondial, le poids de son propre bilan à la tête de la FIFA rend inévitablement la leçon beaucoup plus difficile à entendre et, surtout, particulièrement ironique. The post Mondial 2026 : Sepp Blatter attaque Infantino, des critiques qui ne manquent pas d’ironie appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Revue de presse de ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026
items.description: Voici les principaux titres développés par la presse nationale de ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026 Le Matin Omar Zniber plaide à Genève pour un renforcement de la coopération commerciale Sud-Sud La coopération commerciale Sud-Sud dans le cadre du Système global de préférences commerciales entre pays en développement (GSTP) est « plus que jamais essentielle » à un moment où « les flux commerciaux se redessinent et où le système commercial multilatéral fondé sur des règles est soumis à de fortes tensions », a souligné, lundi à Genève, l’ambassadeur représentant permanent du Royaume du Maroc auprès de l’ONU-Genève, Omar Zniber. Présidant les travaux de la 34e session du Comité des participants du GSTP, M. Zniber a indiqué que cette réunion constitue une opportunité pour réfléchir aux défis auxquels les économies des pays en développement sont confrontées dans le contexte actuel du commerce mondial, ainsi qu’aux moyens de tirer parti de la coopération commerciale Sud-Sud afin d’en atténuer les effets négatifs. Assahra Al-Maghribia Rabat accueille les forces maritimes africaines En application des Hautes Instructions de Sa Majesté le Roi, Chef Suprême et Chef d’État-Major Général des Forces Armées Royales, le Sommet des Forces Maritimes Africaines (AMFS-2026) se tient du 20 au 24 juillet à Rabat. Organisé par la Marine royale, en partenariat avec le Commandement de la Composante des Forces Navales des États-Unis en Europe et en Afrique (NAVAF) et le Commandement des Forces des Marines des États-Unis en Europe et en Afrique (MARFORAF), ce Sommet constitue un cadre privilégié d’échanges consacré aux enjeux de la sécurité maritime et au renforcement de la coopération régionale entre les responsables africains et leurs homologues américains, indique un communiqué de l’État-major Général des Forces Armées Royales. Le président de la CUA salue l’engagement du Maroc en faveur de l’action africaine commune Le président de la Commission de l’Union africaine (CUA), Mahmoud Ali Youssouf, a salué, lundi, l’engagement du Maroc en faveur de l’action africaine commune. Lors d’un entretien au siège de l’organisation panafricaine à Addis-Abeba avec l’ambassadeur, représentant permanent du Royaume auprès de l’UA et de la CEA-ONU, Mohamed Arrouchi, M. Ali Youssouf a salué « l’engagement constructif et les précieuses contributions du Maroc aux travaux de l’UA ». Annahar Al-Maghribia Le Maroc accélère la mise à niveau de l’industrie aéronautique et vise des investissements de haute valeur ajoutée Le Maroc prend part au Farnborough International Airshow 2026, qui s’est ouvert lundi à Farenborough (100 KM au sud-ouest de Londres), avec l’ambition de renforcer son positionnement en tant que plateforme aéronautique de référence. La participation du Maroc au Salon de Farnborough vient ainsi conforter le positionnement du Royaume parmi les hubs aéronautiques mondiaux de référence. Elle reflète la dynamique soutenue d’un secteur en forte croissance, portée par la Vision Éclairée de Sa Majesté le Roi Mohammed VI, plaçant l’industrialisation, l’innovation et le développement des compétences au cœur de la transformation économique du Royaume. Assahra Al-Maghribia Marlaska se félicite de « l’excellente » coopération entre le Maroc et l’Espagne dans le domaine de la Sécurité routière Le ministre espagnol de l’Intérieur, Fernando Grande-Marlaska, a salué, lundi à New York, « l’excellente » coopération entre le Maroc et l’Espagne en matière de sécurité routière. « La coopération et la coordination entre l’Espagne et le Maroc dans ce domaine sont excellentes », a déclaré M. Marlaska à la MAP en marge d’une réunion tenue au siège des Nations Unies sur les progrès et les responsabilités des pays en vue de réduire de moitié le nombre de décès dus aux accidents de la route d’ici à 2030. Al-Haraka Rabat: lancement du Programme d’appui à l’agriculture solidaire inclusive des femmes et des jeunes Le Programme d’appui à l’agriculture solidaire inclusive des femmes et des jeunes (PAASIFEJ) a été lancé, lundi à Rabat, lors d’un séminaire tenu par l’Agence pour le Développement Agricole (ADA), en coordination avec Banque Africaine de Développement (BAD). S’inscrivant dans la dynamique des grands chantiers de développement de l’agriculture solidaire, cette initiative constitue un levier stratégique de la mise en œuvre de la stratégie nationale « Génération Green 2020-2030 ». Doté d’un financement de 216,3 millions d’euros (M€) dont 100 M€ accordé par la BAD, le programme sera déployé sur une période de cinq ans (2025-2030) dans sept régions du Royaume à savoir, Tanger-Tétouan-Al Hoceima, l’Oriental, Fès-Meknès, Béni Mellal-Khénifra, Marrakech-Safi, Drâa-Tafilalet, Souss-Massa. Forte croissance et baisse de l’inflation Selon le rapport de Bank Al-Maghrib (BAM), l’économie marocaine a connu en 2025 un taux de croissance de 4,9%, soit l’un des meilleurs enregistrés lors des dernières années, grâce essentiellement à la hausse des investissements et la continuité des grands projets. En revanche, l’inflation poursuit son recul pour s’établir à 0,8%, un niveau qui traduit la réussite de la politique monétaire à endiguer les pressions inflationnistes après des années marquées par la hausse des prix des produits de base et de l’énergie. De même, BAM a poursuivi sa démarche facilitatrice en procédant à une baisse de son taux directeur à 2,25%, dans le but de promouvoir l’investissement et simplifier l’accès au financement, notamment pour les petites et moyennes entreprises. The post Revue de presse de ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026 appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: L’Algérie enquête sur l’origine d’une vague d’incendies inédite
items.description: Six morts selon un premier bilan officiel, une « véritable catastrophe » évitée mardi à Seraidi sur les hauteurs d’Annaba, des habitants et des malades évacués, des milliers d’hectares réduits en cendre : depuis le début de l’été, l’Algérie fait face à une vague d’incendies d’une ampleur inédite de par son étendue géographique. Les sapeurs-pompiers sont engagés dans une bataille sans fin contre les feux de forêt . La canicule et les vents violents qui soufflent sur certaines régions rendent difficile leur tâche. À Bejaia, des températures de 55° C ont été relevées sur les lieux des incendies. A chaque fois qu’un incendie est éteint après des efforts colossaux, d’autres se déclenchent, ce qui met à rude épreuve le dispositif national de lutte anti-incendie. L’éparpillement des incendies sur une trentaine de wilayas du nord complique davantage la situation et ne permet pas aux unités de la Protection civile de prêter main forte aux régions les plus touchées. La multiplication des feux de forêt pose la question de leur origine, criminelle ou non. En visite mardi soir à Annaba où un incendie d’une grande ampleur a obligé les autorités à évacuer d’urgence des malades de l’hôpital de réduction fonctionnelle de Seraidi et des habitants de cette ville perchée sur les hauteurs du massif de l'Edough à 850 mètres d'altitude, le ministre de l’Intérieur et des Transports, Saïd Sayoud, a annoncé deux mesures phares. Algérie : 1.555 incendies enregistrés depuis le 8 juillet La première est l’engagement de l’Etat à indemniser tous les sinistrés des incendies. Rien qu’à Seraidi, 150 habitations ont été endommagées par les flammes mardi, selon le ministre. La seconde annonce faite par Saïd Sayoud est la « poursuite des enquêtes sécuritaires pour déterminer les causes de ces incendies », a-t-il dit. « Nous avons enregistré 160 incendies depuis la fin du week-end dernier au niveau des wilayas du nord. Les enquêtes des services de sécurité se poursuivent pour déterminer si ces incendies sont d’origine criminelle ou naturelle », a déclaré Saïd Sayoud. Dans la journée de mardi, la Protection civile a enregistré 170 incendies dans 19 wilayas du pays. Les pompiers ont réussi à éteindre 104 alors que 66 sont toujours en activité, selon la même source. Et depuis le 8 juillet, 1555 feux de forêt ont été enregistrés en Algérie, selon la Protection civile. L’article L'Algérie enquête sur l'origine d'une vague d'incendies inédite est apparu en premier sur TSA .

items.title: Iran’s post-war gamble on the young
items.description: Tehran is red and black when I arrive. Millions of people in black mourning attire carried red flags, the stark emblem of blood and martyrdom, seeking revenge for their leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was assassinated on February 28. “We Must Rise,” declare the banners. War songs ricochet off the walls in streets punctuated by elegies on the martyrdom of Imam Husayn and his Companions at Karbala. This is collective grief channelled as state resilience. An elderly supporter I meet, Akbar Ali Jafari, puts it like this: “The war has given the Revolution a new lease on life.” The founding purpose is resurrected and its leadership cast as embattled defenders of the nation rather than an ageing, contested regime. A vehicle carrying coffins of Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and his family members, makes its way as people attend a funeral procession for Khamenei. — Reuters In early July, a towering fist was constructed a few days before the funeral proceedings of the slain leader. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) chose Revolution Square as the spot to assert that the Islamic Republic stood undefeated. What unfolded during the funeral proceedings was a grand spectacle of unity and continuity orchestrated as much for domestic consolidation as for Western consumption. A statue symbolising the hand of Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at Enghelab Square in Tehran, Iran. — Reuters The national mood changes The day after the state funeral in July, the scene changed. Art galleries around Enghelab Square reopened, women in t-shirts and jeans sketched with their heads uncovered. In the affluent Tajrish or centuries-old Grand Bazaar, boys and girls sped by on motorbikes or hung around vaping. In Pasdaran, cafes filled with couples holding hands or groups of youngsters talking over each other loudly. I thought of what Mr Jafari had told me earlier, that the Islamic Republic wants to bring the younger generation into its fold. And indeed, I was looking at the evidence in the public sphere. Restrictions on dress codes appeared to have been further relaxed along with those that regulated music and mixed gatherings outdoors. This was a different Iran from the one just four years ago, managed by a morality police or Gasht-e-Ershad Guidance Patrol force set up during the Ahmadinejad presidency in 2005 to guard the nation’s chastity. But the force was suspended after Mahsa Amini’s death in custody was met with nationwide protests in 2022. Women ripped off their headscarves as they rallied to the cry, “Women, Life, Freedom” in the face of a crackdown. “Those were horrible days,” recalls 24-year-old activist Maryam Rezaei, who works as a manager at a private business. “We used encrypted WhatsApp and Telegram messages to coordinate protests. My friends were detained by the notorious Guidance Patrol and sent to re-education centres. Now there are no white vans with dark green stripes chasing you in public places.” Iranian women walk past a banner with a picture of the former Chief of Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces, Mohammad Bagheri, in Tehran, Iran, July 20, 2026. — Reuters Anti- and pro-government Iranians have a profound connection to their culture and see Iran as a civilizational state, rooted in thousands of years of history. Its complex layers include the Shia Islamic identity, which merges with Persian heritage as opposed to supplanting it. Pre-Islamic heritage is preserved through festivals such as Nowruz, the Iranian New Year, celebrated to welcome spring, along with the winter solstice or Yalda Night and the fire festival Chaharshanbeh Suri. Ferdowsi, the legendary 11th-century poet’s work, Shahnameh , Republicis regarded as a cornerstone of nationhood. So embedded is this pride that the clergy, despite its ideological leanings, has learned to accommodate it and even use it to strengthen nationalist sentiment. The US-Israel war on Iran strengthened the expression of this identity. I speak to Mohammad Mehdi, a young man who runs a handicraft shop with his brother, to gain a sense of the mood. He and his friends heard Trump declare that their civilization should be “erased” and watched America and Israel bomb centuries-old monuments in his hometown of Isfahan, known as Nesf-e-Jahan or ‘Half the World’ for its art, culture, and beauty. And so when there were threats to blow up bridges and power grids, Mehdi and his friends would go to symbolically defend them. “Iran isn’t Venezuela,” he asserts. “We will fight back if they attack us again. We will defeat them first, and then we can fight our internal battles.“ A billboard depicting U.S. President Donald Trump laid in a coffin on a building in Tehran, Iran. — Reuters Women like Maryam and her friend Fatemeh Ahmadi are the digitally native generation which powered anti-regime demonstrations earlier this year. In a paradoxical realignment, however, they are the very same demographic that has now rallied around the state and flag since the US-Israel war on Iran broke out. “How could we support the invading forces?” says Fatemeh. “We are a proud nation, and we back the Islamic Republic for standing firm against the aggression of the US and Israel.” These young Iranians see the government as safeguarding their history and civilisation and are responding to calls for national solidarity, survival instinct, and civilizational pride. Social media was flooded with young people taking pride in Iran’s resilience and defiance in the face of war, with the ‘Lego videos’ by Explosive Media going viral. Faced with an existential external threat, the anger of ‘People vs the Regime’ was supplanted by the hostility of ‘the Aggressor vs Iran’. People management In the backdrop of the war, the softening of social strictures could be a sign of strategic recalibration. How will the government manage the youth, who have demonstrated nationalist loyalty during war, but whose social and cultural values are fundamentally at odds with the theocratic system? At a roadside cafe, this is being discussed by history buff Ali Reza, who is not sure whether the pattern has staying power. “It’s too early to predict whether this change is temporary or will get entrenched,” he says. “They might allow young people to dress with more freedom, listen to their music, and mingle in public, as long as they do not organise politically.” Such a strategy would, in effect, separate lifestyle freedom from political freedom and trade the visibility of social control for the stability of political control. But people close to official circles point out unspoken fears about infiltration, spurred further by US attempts at fomenting uprisings in the wake of the attacks. It is conceivable, though speculative, that the government will relax moral codes for public spaces, but ramp up digital and physical surveillance to detect dissent and espionage. People walk in the Grand Bazaar in Tehran, Iran. — Reuters Beyond the question of lifestyle freedoms lies a deeper dividing line between the revolutionary guards and the clergy who emerged together from the 1979 revolution. The clergy provided ideological direction and the guards the muscle. By the eve of this war, however, the Revolutionary Guards had consolidated its role as the regime’s economic, military, security and regional backbone. The clergy, by contrast, was confronted with its waning authority as it lost ground to the youth in the culture wars. Shielding the people from the US attacks has made the IRGC the guarantor of national survival and repositioned it as the centre of gravity. It has experienced this ascendancy in the past during an earlier national security crisis. After the Iran-Iraq war, the force’s prestige, resources and autonomy massively expanded. Historically, the IRGC has treated social control less as a doctrinal mission and more as an operational necessity, and any opposition as a security vulnerability. How the clergy responds to easing of social controls could be a potential point of friction. It may cause disquiet within the clerical establishment, between the pragmatists who accept the relaxation as a safety valve, and the conservatives who may see it as a surrender and doctrinal retreat. If the internal distribution of political capital and power changes in the post-war period, the IRGC will hold the decisive veto. Follow the money Young people point out, however, that lifestyle freedoms are not their only or greatest concern. Social change alone cannot placate any demographic group in Iran, including the 60per cent youth, because the economy eclipses it. “Life isn’t easy, with soaring inflation strangling low-income families,” says Fatemeh. “Even the middle class is feeling the heat. With an uncertain future, some even feel they cannot breathe freely.” Many young Iranians say the economy is hollowing the country out. Inflation hovers at 80pc, the highest since World War II, and unemployment is a palpable fear. They highlight the need to find an exit from global isolation and sanctions. “No family can survive on a single person’s earnings. My father works as a transporter. I study but work part-time, my sister also earns, only then we can afford a living in Tehran,” says 21-year-old Ali Vahidi, an engineering student. “Daily life is so tough, how then do I dream of a future?” The hardline Revolutionary Guards, however, seem to believe time is on their side. They appear convinced that a prolonged war with “untrustworthy enemies” could yield further geo-strategic advantages: weakening the US security umbrella in the Gulf, providing a safety valve for allies such as the Houthis in Yemen and Hezbollah in Lebanon, and cementing Iran’s emergence as a regional power. They believe Iran as ‘victorious’ and control of the Strait of Hormuz can provide them a renewed economic lifeline. The narrow sea passage, which controls one-fifth of global oil and energy supplies, is a double-edged sword; if it strangles the world economy, then it will have rebound effects on Iran’s economy as well. After a week in the city, as I head back to Tehran’s Imam Khomeini Airport, the changing images of Iran’s history are assembled into a photomosaic-in-process, where the dominant is still not clear. The seeds of a new social contract may have been planted, but the terms are still being written.

items.title: When Chairman Wontumi’s 20-year sentence marks a turning point in the fight against political impunity
items.description: There is an old legal truth that deserves renewed attention : “Justice delayed is not justice denied where the law permits prosecution, but justice obstructed by power is a betrayal of the Republic.” Chairman Wontumi’s conviction is therefore not merely about one individual. It is an indictment of a culture that believed political influence could permanently silence the law. For years, Ghana watched forests disappear, rivers turn brown, communities flood, and livelihoods vanish beneath the greed of illegal mining. Petitions after petitions were reportedly submitted. Warnings were allegedly issued. Yet decisive enforcement appeared conspicuously absent. The question is not whether government officials knew illegal mining existed. The more uncomfortable question is what they did with that knowledge. If petitions concerning Akonta Mining reached the highest levels of the previous administration, then history will judge not only those accused of illegal mining but also those who chose hesitation over constitutional duty. A government that preaches the rule of law while allowing influential allies to enjoy what many perceived as political immunity weakens public confidence in justice itself. Former President Nana Akufo-Addo and former Vice President Dr. Mahamudu Bawumia cannot simply escape legitimate public scrutiny over allegations that repeated concerns about illegal mining involving politically exposed persons failed to receive timely and decisive action. Hence the nickname “ Mr clearing agent” . Leadership carries responsibility not only for acts committed but also for opportunities ignored. The High Court has now demonstrated something profoundly important: no political office, party title or financial influence should place anyone above the law. As for the critics of the decision, their arguments speak for themselves, and the less said about them, the better. It is obvious the court’s ruling is based on the evidence and the law, not politics. After reading the judgment several times, I am convinced by the judge’s reasoning, especially the circumstantial evidence and the cross-examination of the defence witness. In my view, attempts to politicise the case and the opposition’s press conference fail to address the legal issues in the judgment. Justice Audrey Kocuvie-Tay deserves commendation for discharging her constitutional mandate without apparent regard for the political stature of the accused. Delivering judgment against an ordinary citizen requires legal competence. Delivering judgment against a politically influential figure demands something more than judicial courage. Courts exist precisely for moments when power expects exemption. Predictably, the conviction has generated accusations of selective justice. Equality before the law does not require every suspect to be prosecuted simultaneously. Criminal investigations mature at different times, evidence is gathered at different speeds, and prosecutions proceed according to available proof. The constitutional guarantee is equal application of the law, not synchronised arrests. However, those defending the judgment must also embrace the same principle consistently. If investigations reportedly exist concerning persons associated with the current administration or any other political party, they must proceed with equal determination. Justice cannot become a revolving door that prosecutes yesterday’s favourites while protecting today’s allies. The law must remain gloriously indifferent to party colours. Illegal mining is not an NPP crime or an NDC crime. It is a crime against Ghana. Every poisoned river denies children safe drinking water. Every destroyed forest mortgages the country’s environmental future. Samreboi community pays the price for someone’s unlawful profit. Nature conducts no political registration before delivering disaster. Floodwaters do not ask whether victims voted NPP, NDC or any other party. Environmental destruction is ruthlessly non-partisan. Some people in the NPP seem determined to create more disaffection for their own party than their political opponents ever could. How on earth do you tell Ghanaians that if you return to power, Chairman Wontumi will simply be set free? Then others chime in with the familiar “the tables will turn” refrain, as though justice is a football match with home and away fixtures. Such utterances are not only childish but very damaging to their party. If you genuinely believe there are legal grounds, then support an appeal and seek a reduction of the sentence through the courts. That is how the rule of law works. How do you expect the average Ghanaian to willingly vote for a message that appears to put political power above due process? In the end, the electorate will decide. We wish you all the best. But the hard truth is whatever the outcome on appeal, one lesson should already be unmistakably clear: political influence has an expiry date; accountability does not. The true measure of this moment will not be whether one prominent individual was convicted. It will be whether Ghana finally develops the institutional courage to pursue every alleged illegal mining kingpin, regardless of wealth, office, surname or political affiliation. Until then, prosecutions risk appearing episodic rather than transformative. The Republic cannot afford selective outrage or selective justice. It needs selective fear by every public official, financier and politically connected individual contemplating environmental crime. Fear that one day the Constitution, the courts and the evidence will meet. At the very least, it is a matter of public record that President Mahama’s first administration prosecuted some of its own officials. There was even a deputy minister who expressed a personal wish and was subsequently dismissed from office. For too long, Ghana’s forests have carried the scars of human greed while politics supplied convenient insurance policies. If this judgment marks the cancellation of those policies, then history may remember it not simply as the conviction of one man, but as the day the law finally found its voice and reminded the powerful that the Republic belongs not to politicians, but to justice.

items.title: DJ Chicken hails Seyi Tinubu as ‘Daddy’ after release from prison
items.description: Popular Nigerian content creator Ademola Abiodun, also known as DJ Chicken, has stirred reactions on social media after describing Seyi Tinubu, the son of President Bola Tinubu, as ‘daddy’ shortly after regaining his freedom. The entertainer, who was released from the Kirikiri Correctional Centre on Tuesday, July 21, 2026, also shared a photo of Seyi Tinubu on his Instagram Stories with the caption: “Hmm ddddy.” He has also changed his Instagram display picture to one of Seyi. DJ Chicken was released on Tuesday after spending nearly two weeks in prison. Announcing his freedom in a video on social media, the content creator who was seen with his lawyer said “I’m back oo, freedom at last.” “God bless the Nigerian government, God bless Seyi Tinubu, and our president. I love you guys. It is not easy, my fans. People that are free don’t know what God has done for them. Freedom is good”, he said. He was arraigned on a three-count charge bordering on alleged violations of the Cybercrimes Act, breach of peace and making alleged threats to kill Seyi Tinubu. The charges stemmed from a viral video in which DJ Chicken allegedly claimed that President Bola Tinubu would not win re-election in 2027 and was accused of making threats against the President’s son. Following widespread criticism over the video, the content creator withdrew the remarks, explaining that they were intended as a joke to gain attention and increase his online popularity. Despite the apology, he was arrested and brought before the court. Vanguard News The post DJ Chicken hails Seyi Tinubu as ‘Daddy’ after release from prison appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: PFIPC scandal: Apologise and resign now, Atiku tackles Tinubu
items.description: …Cites conflicting CBN, OAGF accounts By Omeiza Ajayi, Abuja Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar has told President Bola Tinubu to apologise to Nigerians and resign over what he described as a cover-up surrounding the Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council PFIPC accounts scandal. His demand followed conflicting testimonies by the Central Bank of Nigeria CBN and the Office of the Accountant-General of the Federation OAGF. In a statement on Wednesday by his Senior Special Assistant on Public Communication, Phrank Shaibu, the Presidential Candidate of the African Democratic Congress ADC said the CBN’s testimony before the House of Representatives fundamentally contradicts the narrative earlier advanced by the OAGF, plunging the Tinubu administration into one of the gravest credibility crises in Nigeria’s democratic history. He insisted that the president owed Nigerians an apology and had lost the moral authority to remain in office if he failed to act. “President Tinubu owes Nigerians an unreserved apology for presiding over this unprecedented collapse of public accountability. If he cannot immediately restore public confidence through a genuinely independent investigation and ensure that everyone responsible is held to account, then he has forfeited the moral authority to continue in office. The honourable course is simple: apologise to Nigerians and resign,” he declared. The former Vice President said he could only sympathise with President Tinubu, adding that no serious leader needed a clairvoyant to know that his administration was trapped in a web of deception. “When two of your most sensitive financial and monetary authorities publicly contradict each other on the same transaction, no serious leader needs a clairvoyant to know that his administration is trapped in a web of deception and a profound crisis of credibility. The President cannot continue to watch from the sidelines while his government tears itself apart with conflicting narratives,” he said. Atiku insisted that Tinubu must clarify which of the two accounts he believed, warning that his continued silence deepened public suspicion. “If he believes neither, why has he not acted? Every day he delays ordering a truly independent investigation, he deepens public suspicion that preserving his administration’s image has taken precedence over establishing the truth,” he said. He also faulted the attorney-general of the Federation for maintaining silence on the matter, describing it as a compounding factor in the crisis. He called on the National Assembly to immediately summon the Accountant-General of the Federation and the Governor of the Central Bank to appear together before its investigative committee under oath so that Nigerians could finally hear one truthful account of the transaction. “The PFIPC scandal has ceased to be merely about disputed accounts. It has become a referendum on President Tinubu’s integrity and competence. If a single individual could allegedly orchestrate a scheme that has thrown two of Nigeria’s most critical financial and monetary authorities into public contradiction, Nigerians are entitled to ask how many other abuses may have escaped detection because of weak oversight and institutional failure,” he said. The post PFIPC scandal: Apologise and resign now, Atiku tackles Tinubu appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Contrôle des finances publiques : renforcement des compétences des magistrats financiers
items.description: La capitale sénégalaise accueille, depuis hier, 21 juillet 2026, le premier séminaire professionnel de la Commission de renforcement de capacités de l’Organisation des institutions supérieures de contrôle ayant des compétences juridictionnelles (Jurisai). Cette rencontre de trois jours est placée sur le thème de la gestion des compétences des professionnels des fonctions juridictionnelles. « Gestion des compétences du professionnel des fonctions juridictionnelles : défis et perspectives ». Tel est le thème du premier séminaire professionnel organisé par la Cour des Comptes du Sénégal, en collaboration avec l’Organisation des institutions supérieures de contrôle des finances publiques à fonctions juridictionnelles (Jurisai). La rencontre, ouverte à Dakar, et retransmise en vidéo-conférence, se poursuit jusqu'au 23 juillet 2026. Elle réunit des chefs d'Institutions supérieures de contrôle (Isc), des procureurs généraux, ainsi que de nombreux magistrats du siège et du ministère public. Selon le Premier président de la Cour des comptes, Abdoul Madjib Guèye, cet évènement s’inscrit « dans le prolongement direct du mandat confié à la juridiction sénégalaise pour présider la Commission de renforcement de capacités » mise en place par l’Assemblée générale constitutive de Jurisai. Il a souligné que « les fonctions juridictionnelles exercées par nos institutions ne sont pas des attributions ordinaires : elles touchent directement aux droits, à la situation patrimoniale et, parfois, à la carrière des justiciables de nos juridictions financières ». M. Guèye a rappelé que la légitimité d’une Isc reposait avant tout sur « des femmes et des hommes compétents, agissant selon des processus de qualité au sein d’une institution résolument professionnalisée ». D’après le Premier président de la Cour des comptes, malgré l’existence de normes internationales, la situation sur le terrain demeure contrastée d'un pays à l'autre. Il souligne plusieurs défis persistants au sein des Isc : une formalisation encore incomplète des cadres de compétences, des programmes de développement professionnel parfois déconnectés des réalités du terrain, ainsi que des mécanismes d’évaluation des performances à consolider. Face à ces constats, Emmanuelle Borrel, représentante de Jurisai, soutient que la gestion des compétences constitue aujourd'hui « un véritable levier stratégique de gouvernance ». Elle estime d'ailleurs que ce séminaire doit servir de base référentielle à l'ensemble des Isc dotées de fonctions juridictionnelles, afin qu'elles puissent mener à bien l'intégralité de leurs missions, qu'elles soient juridictionnelles ou non. Fatou SY

items.title: Europol : la menace terroriste s’amplifie et change d’algorithme
items.description: « Le terrorisme en Europe entre dans une nouvelle phase », previent Europol dans son rapport annuel publie en juillet. En 2025, dix etats membres ont signale 45 attaques : 22 commises, 20 dejouees et trois ayant echoue. Parallelement, 486 suspects ont ete interpelles dans 21 pays, contre 449 en 2024. Des djihadistes aux communautes de l'ultraviolence La permanence du danger djihadiste reste la premiere conclusion. Le djihadisme est en cause dans 24 des 45 attaques recensees et 347 arrestations, soit plus de 71 % du total. Ses attentats ont cause cinq des six morts et 81 blesses. Le « nouveau visage », decrit par Europol, ne remplace donc pas les menaces anciennes : il s'y ajoute, les brouille et complique leur detection, alors que quelques mois plus tot, Europol interpellait sur le risque de voir se developper un terrorisme robotique . Le changement tient d'abord au profil des personnes interpellees. Leur âge moyen etait de 27 ans, 130 avaient 18 ans ou moins et le plus jeune seulement 12 ans. Europol observe chez certains jeunes radicalises une connaissance superficielle des doctrines, remplacee par un melange instable de djihadisme, neonazisme, misogynie, nihilisme, ressentiments personnels ou fascination pour les tueries de masse. Terrorisme dans l'UE : Europol dresse le portrait d'une menace de plus en plus fragmentée Europol a publié cette semaine son rapport annuel TE-SAT 2026 (European Union Terrorism Situation and Trend Report), qui dresse le bilan de la menace terroriste dans l'Union européenne pour… pic.twitter.com/4ZhRXb2fXw — FT Osint (@FTOsint) July 16, 2026 Les plateformes accelerent cette recomposition. Reseaux sociaux, messageries securisees, forums, services decentralises et univers lies aux jeux video permettent de recruter, de diffuser des modes operatoires et de passer plus rapidement de la consommation d'images a la violence reelle. La « gamification » : classements, defis et recompenses, contribuerait a banaliser les contenus extremes et a effacer la frontiere entre activite numerique et passage a l'acte. Le rapport s'attarde notamment sur le « Com network », ecosysteme mouvant associant cyberattaques, extorsion sexuelle de mineures et violences physiques, jusqu'aux incendies, agressions au couteau ou meurtres. Aucune ideologie unique ne domine cet univers : le statut dans le groupe et la recherche de reconnaissance peuvent suffire a mobiliser certains participants. Sur LinkedIn, Europol resume cette mutation par une formule : la violence devient un instrument «d'identite, de reconnaissance et d'appartenance». Pour les services antiterroristes, surveiller les organisations structurees ne suffit plus lorsque la menace naît aussi d'individus isoles, naviguant d'une doctrine a l'autre au gre des algorithmes.

items.title: ADRA Ghana donates relief items, calls for sustainable recovery support for June 29 flood victims
items.description: The Adventist Development and Relief Agency (ADRA) Ghana has called for a shift from emergency relief to long-term recovery support for families affected by the June 29 floods, saying many victims now face the challenge of rebuilding their homes and livelihoods. The organisation appealed while presenting relief items to the National Disaster Management Organisation (NADMO) in Accra to support ongoing assistance for flood victims. Citing NADMO data, ADRA Ghana said nearly 90,000 households were affected by the floods, leaving many families displaced after losing homes, personal belongings and sources of income. Speaking at the presentation, ADRA Ghana Country Director, Dr Isaac Kankam-Boadu, said although emergency relief remains critical, sustainable recovery should focus on helping affected families regain their livelihoods. “Our mandate calls us to action, and that is why we have mobilised resources from our international office, our regional office and within the Seventh-day Adventist Church in Ghana to support victims of the floods,” he said. Dr Kankam-Boadu disclosed that ADRA Ghana had mobilised about US$20,000, equivalent to approximately GH¢224,000, to procure relief items, including toiletries, student mattresses, detergents, washing powder and other household essentials identified by NADMO as urgent needs. He said the organisation intends to work beyond relief distribution by partnering with NADMO and development partners to help affected families rebuild their livelihoods. “After this presentation, we will engage our donor partners and, once NADMO identifies the beneficiaries, work collaboratively to provide skills training and livelihood support so affected victims can generate income and bounce back to their former state,” he said. He added that ADRA Ghana is also prepared to deploy volunteers to provide psychosocial support and health education during community-level relief distribution. Dr Kankam-Boadu attributed the growing severity of flooding in Ghana to the combined effects of climate change and human activities. “Climate change is leading to higher rainfall, while the rapid concretisation of our urban areas reduces the ability of the soil to absorb excess water. At the same time, citizens must be educated on how to manage the environment to reduce flood risks,” he stated. Receiving the items on behalf of the government, NADMO Deputy Director-General in charge of Technical, Alhaji Ahmed-Rufai Afarahim, expressed appreciation to ADRA Ghana and assured that the relief items would reach the affected communities. “We promise these items will get to the victims of the June 29 flood disaster,” he said. He described the donation as a demonstration of national solidarity, noting that disasters affect people regardless of their religion or background. “The disaster doesn’t know who is a Christian and who is a Muslim. We thank ADRA for what they have done,” he added. The June 29 floods affected several communities across Ghana, causing widespread destruction and displacing thousands of households. As recovery efforts continue, ADRA Ghana says sustained partnerships and livelihood support will be critical to helping affected families rebuild their lives.

items.title: Rain-related fatalities during ongoing monsoon spell rise to 18 in KP
items.description: LOWER SOUTH WAZIRISTAN: The death toll from rain-related incidents during the ongoing monsoon spell and flash floods in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa has risen to 18. According to the Pakistan Meteorological Department, the current rain spell, which began on July 19, is expected to last till July 23 in KP. The latest rain-related fatalities in the province were reported from Upper South Waziristan, where four people were killed in a roof collapse incident, Additional Deputy Commissioner Fazal Wadood told Dawn . He said the incident occurred in the Bangiwala village on the outskirts of Sararogha tehsil during heavy rainfall. The victims belonged to the same family, including a couple and their son and daughter, he added. Separately, a report released by the Provincial Disaster Management Authority (PDMA) this morning said that 14 people had died and 19 had been injured in rain-related incidents across the province since July 19. The report did not mention the South Waziristan fatalities, which take the death toll recorded between July 19 and July 22 thus far to 18. The authority’s report said 22 houses had been partially damaged in rain-related incidents and six destroyed. The PDMA said in a statement that water levels and flows in all rivers and streams across the province were currently normal, and all district administrations had been directed to remain on alert. It added that people may call the helpline 1700 for information about weather conditions or in case of any untoward incident. Landslides block Wana-Gomal Zam Road Heavy rains have also disrupted routine life in Lower South Waziristan, with landslides blocking the Wana-Gomal Zam Road — one of the most important trade and communication routes in the region. There are also reports of flash floods damaging agricultural land in several areas. A district administration official confirmed the road blockage to Dawn . Several residents and commuters told Dawn over the phone that large boulders had fallen from mountains at several points along the road, while deep potholes had developed at various locations. They said several culverts, bridges and protection walls had also been damaged, making travel difficult and risky for motorists. They called on the authorities to take immediate steps to reopen the road and restore traffic. Meanwhile, Ahmadzai Wazir tribal elders residing in the region, while speaking to Dawn , urged Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and other relevant authorities to take immediate measures for the reconstruction of the Wana-Gomal Zam Road. The elders said the road had been in disrepair for a long time and was often closed even after moderate rainfall because of its poor condition. In another development, there were also reports of flash floods in various areas of Lower South Waziristan. District administration officials told Dawn that high flows were reported in the Tanai and Spin streams following the rainfall. Govt reviews monsoon preparedness Planning, Development and Special Initiatives Minister Ahsan Iqbal posted on X that he had chaired the third meeting of the Emergency Response Committee at the National Emergencies Operations Centre (NEOC) on Wednesday to review the country’s preparedness for the ongoing monsoon season. The minister said the meeting, attended by National Disaster Management Authority Chairman Lt Gen Inam Haider Malik and representatives of relevant federal ministries, reviewed provincial preparedness, relief supplies and drainage systems in detail. “The NEOC is monitoring the weather situation round the clock, and I have issued strict instructions to all departments, including the Water and Power Development Authority, […] to remain on high alert,” the minister said. “Our utmost priority is to protect the life of every citizen, safeguard vulnerable areas, and ensure swift relief operations in any emergency situation,” he said.
