items.title: ‘Dead on arrival’- Ganduje says Obi-Kwankwaso ticket will fail in Kano
items.description: Former National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Abdullahi Ganduje, has dismissed the presidential ticket of Peter Obi and Rabiu Kwankwaso on the platform of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), declaring that it would not succeed in Kano State in the 2027 general election. Speaking with journalists on Tuesday, the former Kano State governor said the ticket lacked the political strength and governance credentials needed to win the support of Kano voters. “I don’t think Peter Obi and Kwankwaso’s ticket will succeed in Kano. It will not succeed. In fact, it is dead on arrival,” Ganduje said. He questioned Obi’s performance during his tenure as governor of Anambra State, arguing that the former governor had not demonstrated the leadership record required to govern Nigeria. “Because Peter Obi was a governor in the South-East geopolitical zone, a governor in Anambra State. What did he do as a governor? What legacy did he leave as a governor?” he asked. “And from that, what other responsibility in governance did he hold that he made a mark to the extent that we believe he will be able to manage this country effectively and efficiently?” Ganduje also criticised Kwankwaso’s political career, noting that unlike some former governors of Kano, he did not secure two consecutive terms in office. “Kwankwaso too, he was a governor of Kano State. He was not able to be a governor back-to-back,” he said. “So far, the two other governors in Kano were able to win elections back-to-back. Mallam Ibrahim Shekarau was able to spend eight years straight. Myself, I was able to spend eight years straight.” The former APC chairman further argued that Kwankwaso had not left behind a legacy capable of convincing Kano residents or Nigerians that he would make an effective vice-president. “So I don’t think, even in Kano, what legacy specifically can you say that he has left in Kano that will convince the people of Kano, will convince the people of Nigeria that he will be a successful vice-president of the Federal Republic of Nigeria?” Ganduje said. The post ‘Dead on arrival’- Ganduje says Obi-Kwankwaso ticket will fail in Kano appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Women, agents of change: My take from Vancouver Zonta Convention
items.description: The more one thinks about it, the more one realises that there is no argument that women, for sure, are change agents. No matter which culture or part of the world one puts this assertion to the test, there would never be any doubt that wherever women thrive, change occurs for good. This distinctive fact was clearly stated and given the thumbs up all the way in Canada, where last week, Zonta International held its 67 th Convention in the vibrant and cosmopolitan city of Vancouver. Zonta convention Zonta International is an organisation of mainly women, with a few male members, in 65 countries across the globe. They work together to progress the human rights of women and girls, while unveiling opportunities that are and can be available for women and girls, particularly in disadvantaged communities in every part of the world. At the opening ceremony of the five-day convention, the guest speaker, Sally Armstrong, a renowned journalist, a global war correspondent focusing on women’s issues, a humanitarian and author, brought home to the over 300 Zonta International members the positive influence women make as catalysts for change, both at global and local levels. While urging members of Zonta International everywhere to strive to be those exemplary change makers in their communities, she asserted that strong communities have been built by stronger women. She added that women’s experiences from labour at childbirth alone encourage them in their beliefs that with perseverance and hard pushing, the end result is always beautiful and worth the effort. She commended the hard work of women in organisations such as Zonta International, and invited women all over the world, as change makers, to align with Zonta’s zeal and work to break the stigmatisation and stereotyped treatments that societies tag women and girls with. She called on women to help bring closure to the depravity that communities, families, workplaces, educational institutions and others box women and girls into. The message of the renowned, well-travelled Journalist hit the core of my heart. One does not need to look far to see women’s critical role as agents of change in any society. One just have to cast one’s mind back to one’s own home and the things one takes for granted in the home. Pivots Mothers and grandmothers in the majority of homes have become the pivots around which families revolve. For 24 hours, women, well or unwell, strive to ensure that everyone within their confines is comfortable for the better. In the few homes where the mother figure is absent, insecurity dominates. Mothers juggle many roles at home. They are cooks, nurses, teachers, non-ordained priests, cleaners, carers, and whatever role one can think of. They bring positive changes to homes and sometimes even to communities and their churches. In our wider traditional settings, our queen mothers set the pace for peace and positive change. They embody a powerhouse for good advice often tapped into. Their presence alone in a community brings some aura because of the positions they occupy as change makers. At the higher national level, even in our own country, the examples are plenty. Because women see themselves as change makers, they strive to make a difference in their public roles, bringing steadiness and level-headedness. A cursory look worldwide at women who have occupied the highest positions in their countries brings freshness to the debate. Mrs Margaret Thatcher, the former Prime Minister of Britain and the former German Chancellor, Angela Merkel, stand out. Also standing out tall recently is how female heads of Government in parts of the world settled the commotion at a time when the COVID-19 pandemic was wreaking havoc in every part of the world. In Europe, where the pandemic was critically brutal, the countries which had female Presidents and Prime Ministers safely guided their countries through the pandemic with very few deaths, compared to other countries in their region. Who can forget the headlines that hit the world with Germany’s Angela Merkel’s leadership? Who can forget the exceptional management of COVID-19’s impact on countries like Denmark and Finland by their Prime Minister and President, respectively? Both of them were women. The world stood up for them due to the critical changes they brought at a time when fear gripped an entire world. These examples and many more are why an organisation like Zonta International is pushing against societal stigmatisation of women and asking for girls and women everywhere to be given a chance. That chance can only come if equality and human rights of women and girls are prioritised. Zonta International is asking that, to be on a level playing ground, violence and discrimination against girls and women end in the world. Violence like rape, female genital mutilation, and childhood marriage all block the chances of girls and women to education, freedom, better jobs and many other limitations. Yes, through Zonta’s work in world communities, legal, moral and labour changes have occurred in some societies. But much more needs to be achieved. That is why Zonta International says that women are change agents, and their chances of nurturing such potential should not be thwarted by stereotyping, local or international. ****** You can contact the writer at vickywirekoandoh@yahoo.com

items.title: FG lauds Kano Govt for dedicating 32% of budget on education
items.description: The Federal Government has lauded the Kano State Government for allocating 32 per cent of its budget to education. The News Agency of Nigeria (NAN) reports that the Kano State Government allocated a historic N405 billion out of its N1.36 trillion proposed budget for the 2026 fiscal year to the education sector. NAN reports that this follows the state government’s declaration of a state of emergency on education. The Minister of Education, Dr Tunji Alausa, gave the commendation on Tuesday in Kano during the presentation of permanent and pensionable appointment letters to 4,900 newly recruited teachers and the engagement of 3,688 Abba Gida Gida BESDA Cohort V facilitators. He described the feat as a major investment in human capital development and quality education. Alausa described teachers as the most important professionals in any society, saying they play a critical role in shaping future generations and national development. “You have a performing governor who has prioritised education. In three years, Kano has recruited about 16,000 teachers, which is a remarkable investment in human capital development. “Teachers are the most important professionals in any country because good teachers produce good students and responsible citizens,” he said. Alausa urged the newly recruited teachers to justify the confidence reposed in them through dedication, professionalism and commitment to quality teaching. “Much has been given to you, and much is expected. Make the governor proud, make Kano State proud and teach every child with passion and care,” he said. He also commended the state government’s investment in infrastructure, including road construction and installation of solar-powered street lights, describing the projects as evidence of good governance. Earlier, Gov. Abba Kabir-Yusuf said the presentation of appointment letters marked another milestone in his administration’s efforts to reposition the education sector. He said the government recruited more than 13,000 qualified teachers into basic and secondary schools between May 2023 and June 2026. According to him, the latest recruitment of 4,900 teachers and engagement of 3,688 BESDA Cohort V facilitators bring the total number of newly recruited teachers and facilitators under his administration to more than 16,000. Kabir-Yusuf described the exercise as an investment in the future of Kano’s children and reaffirmed his administration’s commitment to improving access to quality education. He said the government had also invested in the construction and renovation of classrooms, provision of school furniture, primary school uniforms , instructional materials, and reopening of 15 boarding schools. Others are: establishment of six technical schools and continuous payment of counterpart funding to access interventions from the Universal Basic Education Commission (UBEC). He said the BESDA facilitators would serve for an initial period of nine months, after which qualified beneficiaries could be considered for permanent appointment based on performance. Also speaking, the Executive Secretary of the Kano State Universal Basic Education Board (SUBEB), Alhaji Yusuf Kabir, said the recruitment followed due process to ensure that only qualified, competent and committed teachers were employed. He said the exercise was guided by transparency, merit and the urgent need to improve teaching and learning outcomes across public schools. He said education should be regarded as a shared responsibility, adding that community ownership of schools would improve learning outcomes and maximise government investments. Also speaking, the Executive Secretary of UBEC, Dr Aisha Garba, described the recruitment as a timely investment in the future of Kano State and commended the governor. “Every appointment letter represented renewed hope for classrooms, communities and children,” she said. Garba noted that UNESCO estimated that more than 44 million teachers were needed globally to bridge the shortage of teachers, adding that Kano had distinguished itself by recruiting over 16,000 teachers in three years. She reaffirmed UBEC’s commitment to supporting Kano in expanding access to quality basic education. Speaking on behalf of the beneficiaries, Abdullahi Musa-Abba thanked the governor for the opportunity and pledged that the newly recruited teachers would discharge their duties diligently and uphold the ethics of the teaching profession. The post FG lauds Kano Govt for dedicating 32% of budget on education appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Ghanaian author’s cancer memoir added to Stanford University library, recognised by US Library of Congress
items.description: Dying Many Times: The Struggles of a Cancer Patient, a memoir and advocacy book by Ghanaian author Benjamin Akyena Brantuo, has achieved a series of significant international milestones after being acquired by Stanford University Libraries in the United States and formally catalogued by the The Library of Congress The achievements represent important recognition for a book that documents the lived experience of Acute Lymphoblastic Leukaemia (ALL) and advocates for improved cancer care, particularly in developing countries. The book, first published in 2021, chronicles the author’s journey alongside his late wife, Patience Brantuo, who died from Acute Lymphoblastic Leukaemia on 2 April 2020 after three years of marriage. Through a deeply personal narrative, the book examines the realities of cancer diagnosis, treatment and patient care while proposing practical recommendations for strengthening healthcare systems. Among its latest achievements: Stanford University Libraries has added Dying Many Times to its permanent collection through its African acquisitions programme, making it available to students, researchers and faculty through the University’s library system. The Library of Congress has assigned the book an official classification within its catalogue for works relating to leukaemia, enabling researchers and libraries worldwide to discover it as a recognised contribution to literature on the disease and the patient experience. The book has been indexed in the WorldCat global bibliographic network, making it discoverable through thousands of library catalogues across the world. The Library of Congress has also established an official Name Authority Record for Benjamin Akyena Brantuo, uniquely identifying him as an author within international library systems and ensuring that his publications are correctly attributed across academic and research institutions. Commenting on the development, Benjamin Akyena Brantuo said: “These milestones are deeply humbling. They are not simply recognition of a book, but recognition of the importance of documenting the experiences of cancer patients and their families. “When my wife, Patience, was receiving treatment at Cape Coast Teaching Hospital, she once held my hand during a prayer session and said, ‘Ben, if I recover from this condition, I will spend the rest of my life serving humanity.’ “Although she did not survive, I believe that dream lives on through this book. If it helps educate healthcare professionals, informs research, inspires better policy or gives hope to even one family facing cancer, then her legacy continues.” According to Irina Trapido, Acting Head of Acquisitions & Collections Services at Stanford University Libraries: “Your book was acquired through our approval plan with one of our African book vendors. Through this arrangement, titles of relevant scholarly and cultural significance from African publishers and authors are supplied to the library for consideration and inclusion in the collection.” Benjamin believes these developments demonstrate that personal experiences, when carefully documented, can contribute meaningfully to academic research, medical education and public policy discussions. Since its publication, Dying Many Times has contributed to national conversations on improving cancer care in Ghana. Around four months after the book’s publication, the National Health Insurance Scheme (NHIS) expanded its coverage to include four childhood cancers – development the author welcomes as an important step forward. However, he believes much more remains to be done to improve access to affordable, timely and patient-centred cancer care. He hopes the book will continue to inform public discussion and support efforts to strengthen cancer diagnosis and treatment, while ensuring that the experiences of patients and their families remain central to ongoing reforms. He is also calling on educational institutions in Ghana to consider the book as recommended reading for students pursuing studies in medicine, nursing, public health and the biomedical sciences. He believes its combination of lived experience and practical recommendations offers valuable insights for future healthcare professionals. Reflecting on the journey, Benjamin expressed gratitude to his family, friends, church members, readers and all those who have supported the book since its publication. He paid special tribute to his son, Elijah, whose patience and understanding made it possible for him to devote countless hours to writing and promoting the book. He also acknowledged the contributions of Buabeng Books, Mr Senyo Hosi and Mr Dennis Adjei-Dwomoh, whose encouragement and support helped bring the project to fruition. “Patience dreamed of serving humanity,” he said. “Today, through this book, I believe that dream continues.”

items.title: Why I’ve never openly endorsed any politician or political party — Falz
items.description: Popular Nigerian rapper and activist, Folarin Falana, popularly known as Falz, has said he has never publicly endorsed any politician or political party, explaining that he remains cautious about lending his support to political figures. Speaking on the need for greater civic participation ahead of future elections, Falz said his advocacy has always centred on encouraging Nigerians to vote rather than campaigning for any candidate. “Just a point of correction: I’ve never, in 2023 or at any point, openly supported any politician or political party. I’ve only actively encouraged people to go out to vote and get their intentions known,” he said. The entertainer explained that his priority is to strengthen Nigeria’s democracy by promoting credible elections and higher voter participation. “What I clamor for is a free and fair democratic process, that people let their intentions be known, and that people go out to vote. These, I think, are very important to strengthen our democracy in this country,” he said. Falz added that he had deliberately avoided endorsing politicians because he believes the country’s political class has largely remained unchanged over the years. “I’ve never actually stuck out my neck for any politician for obvious reasons. We’ve been going in cycles, and it just seems like almost the same crop of people we keep on reshuffling. At the end of the day, it’s tough to even sit anywhere and vouch for a Nigerian politician in 2026,” he said. He, however, noted that he could reconsider his position if he found a candidate he believed in completely. “If I see someone that I feel 100 per cent is the one, maybe I would. But as it stands, I’m just trying to be cautious. I’m trying not to outrightly stick out my neck for anyone,” he added. Falz also dismissed the notion that votes do not count, arguing that politicians would not spend money buying votes if elections were inconsequential. “If it didn’t matter, there wouldn’t be vote-buying if it didn’t matter,” he said, urging Nigerians, particularly young people, to reject voter apathy. The rapper further advocated the live electronic transmission of election results, saying it would improve transparency and credibility. He questioned why the electoral system had yet to fully adopt real-time result transmission despite the huge sums budgeted for elections. According to him, the key lesson from the 2023 elections is the need for a much higher voter turnout, insisting that “if there is an overwhelming majority, it would be incredibly difficult for them to cheat that.” The post Why I’ve never openly endorsed any politician or political party — Falz appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Expertise indépendante et positionnement international de la Tunisie
items.description: Dans un environnement marqué par une concurrence ardue, complexité technique, juridique et réglementaire croissante, le recours à un expert tiers indépendant constitue un facteur essentiel de confiance, de performance et de maîtrise des risques. Par son impartialité, ses compétences reconnues et le respect de règles déontologiques rigoureuses, il contribue à la qualité des décisions, à la prévention et au règlement des différends endogènes et exogènes, au développement des compétences ainsi qu’à la crédibilité des processus de certification des personnes. À travers une analyse de ses fondements et de ses principaux domaines d’intervention, cet article met en évidence la contribution stratégique de l’expertise indépendante à la gouvernance, à l'amélioration continue à la croissance et à la création de valeur pour une meilleure gestion des projets. Taoufik Halila & Mohamed Farid Herelli * L’évolution des métiers, l’accélération des innovations technologiques en général ; l’intelligence artificielle en particulier et les exigences croissantes des parties prenantes rendent indispensable une évaluation objective des compétences et des situations techniques complexes. Dans ce contexte, l’expert tiers indépendant occupe une place stratégique en apportant une analyse fondée sur des faits vérifiables, des compétences reconnues et une indépendance de jugement. Son intervention dépasse désormais le seul cadre de la résolution des différends. Elle contribue à la diffusion des bonnes pratiques, à la prévention des risques, à l’amélioration continue des organisations et au renforcement de la confiance entre les acteurs économiques, institutionnels et judiciaires ce qui constitue une base fondamentale pour la création d’un environnement favorable au développement des investissements et la création de la richesse Cette évolution s'inscrit dans les principes des référentiels internationaux, notamment la norme ISO/IEC 17024, qui fait de l’impartialité, de la compétence et de l’indépendance les fondements d’une certification des personnes dotée d’une fiabilité, cohérence et reconnaissance à l’international. L’expertise indépendante apparaît ainsi comme un levier majeur de gouvernance, de performance et de développement durable. Les fondements de l'expertise indépendante : La crédibilité d'un expert tiers indépendant repose sur trois principes complémentaires : la compétence technique, le respect des règles déontologiques et l'impartialité. L'expertise technique : l’expert fonde son intervention sur une connaissance approfondie de son domaine d’activité, acquise grâce à une formation spécialisée, une expérience significative, une veille permanente des évolutions scientifiques, techniques et réglementaires ainsi qu’une maîtrise des référentiels applicables. Cette expertise lui permet de produire des analyses objectives, argumentées et vérifiables, indispensables à la prise de décision ; Déontologie, impartialité et neutralité : l’expertise indépendante ne peut produire ses effets que si elle s’exerce dans le respect de principes éthiques rigoureux. L’impartialité, l’intégrité, la confidentialité, l’absence de conflit d’intérêts et la transparence constituent les fondements de la crédibilité de l’expert. Contrairement au conseil qui défend les intérêts d’une partie, l’expert tiers intervient exclusivement au service des faits. Sa mission consiste à analyser objectivement une situation, à identifier les causes d’un problème, à proposer des solutions techniquement fondées et à éclairer la décision du donneur d’ordre, du juge ou des parties concernées. Cette posture renforce la confiance dans les conclusions de l’expertise et favorise des décisions plus justes, plus transparentes et plus durables. Les différences conceptuelles entre accréditation, certification et labellisation Expert tiers indépendant et développement des compétences: L’expertise indépendante ne se limite pas à l’évaluation ou au règlement des différends. Chaque mission constitue également une opportunité de transfert de connaissances et de diffusion des bonnes pratiques. Par ses observations et ses recommandations, il contribue à corriger les écarts techniques, à prévenir la répétition des erreurs, à renforcer la culture qualité et à améliorer les compétences individuelles et collectives. Son intervention favorise ainsi l’apprentissage organisationnel et l’amélioration continue. Dans les organismes de certification, les retours d’expérience des experts permettent d’actualiser les référentiels d’évaluation, d’adapter les programmes de formation aux évolutions des métiers et de garantir une meilleure adéquation entre les compétences certifiées et les besoins du terrain. Au-delà de son rôle d’évaluateur, l’expert tiers indépendant devient ainsi un véritable partenaire de la performance durable des organisations. En favorisant la montée en compétences, la maîtrise des risques et la diffusion d’une culture d’excellence, il contribue directement à la compétitivité, à l’innovation et à la confiance des parties prenantes. Contribution de l’expert tiers à la certification des personnes : La certification des personnes repose sur un principe fondamental : la confiance dans l’évaluation. Cette confiance ne peut être garantie que si les évaluateurs disposent des compétences requises, exercent leurs missions en toute indépendance et appliquent des méthodes d’évaluation impartiales et transparentes. Dans ce cadre, l’expert tiers indépendant joue un rôle déterminant. Il veille à l’égalité de traitement entre les candidats, à la cohérence des évaluations et à la crédibilité des certifications délivrées. Son intervention favorise la reconnaissance des compétences par les employeurs, facilite la mobilité professionnelle et renforce la confiance des autorités réglementaires ainsi que des parties prenantes. Par ailleurs et conformément aux principes de la norme ISO/IEC 17024, qui est la norme de référence pour la certification des compétences des personnes, la valeur d’une certification dépend autant de la compétence du candidat que de la qualité, de l’impartialité et de la compétence de l’expert chargé de son évaluation. L’expertise indépendante constitue ainsi un élément essentiel de la fiabilité des dispositifs de certification garantissant que les professionnels certifiés ont été évalués selon un processus rigoureux, impartial et conforme aux bonnes pratiques internationales ce qui résulte d’une part pour les personnes certifiées une reconnaissance des compétences et une meilleure employabilité et d’autre part ,pour les organismes employeurs une réduction des risques liés aux qualifications du personnel et un renforcement de la crédibilité et de la confiance auprès des clients. Principaux domaines d'intervention de l'expertise indépendante : L’expertise indépendante intervient dans de nombreux secteurs où la qualité des décisions repose sur une évaluation objective et impartiale. En médiation, l’expert ou le médiateur indépendant facilite le dialogue entre les parties, identifie les intérêts communs et contribue à la recherche de solutions équilibrées, limitant ainsi le recours à des procédures contentieuses longues et coûteuses. Dans le domaine de l’assurance, son intervention permet d’évaluer objectivement les dommages, d’analyser les causes d'un sinistre et de contribuer à une indemnisation équitable, tout en réduisant les risques de contestation. Le développement des échanges numériques a également renforcé le rôle du tiers de confiance, chargé de garantir l’identité des signataires, l’intégrité des documents, leur horodatage et leur valeur juridique. Cette fonction participe à la sécurisation des transactions électroniques et à la prévention des risques de fraude. Enfin, dans le domaine judiciaire, l’expert apporte au juge les éléments techniques nécessaires à l’appréciation des situations complexes. Son rapport, fondé sur des méthodes reconnues et des données vérifiables, constitue un outil essentiel d’aide à la prise de décision. Par ailleurs, la transformation numérique des procédures d’expertise impose désormais des nouvelles exigences en matière de traçabilité, de sécurité des données, de confidentialité, d’archivage et de respect des délais, ce qui renforce la fiabilité et la qualité des missions engagées. Bénéfices économiques et organisationnels de l’expertise indépendante : Le recours précoce à un expert tiers indépendant constitue un investissement stratégique pour la gestion et le suivi des projets. En apportant une évaluation objective et des recommandations fondées sur des éléments techniques, il contribue à améliorer la qualité des décisions et à réduire les incertitudes, les budgets complémentaires et les délais de mise en service. Cette démarche permet notamment de maîtriser les risques techniques, de limiter les coûts liés aux contentieux et aux non-conformités, de réduire les délais de traitement des différends et d’améliorer la performance globale des projets. Elle favorise également la confiance des clients, des investisseurs, des partenaires et des autorités de contrôle. Les travaux récents consacrés à la gouvernance, à la certification et au règlement des différends convergent vers une même conclusion : la compétence, l’impartialité, la transparence et la responsabilité constituent les fondements de la confiance accordée aux experts et aux organismes tiers. L’expertise indépendante apparaît ainsi comme un levier de création de valeur, de maîtrise des risques et d'amélioration durable de la performance des organisations. Dans un environnement marqué par la complexité, la transformation numérique et des exigences croissantes en matière de transparence, l’expert tiers indépendant s’impose comme un acteur majeur dans la gestion moderne des projets. Par son expertise, son indépendance et son respect des principes déontologiques, il contribue à sécuriser les décisions, à renforcer la confiance entre les parties prenantes et à accompagner le développement des compétences. Son intervention dépasse largement le cadre de la résolution des différends : elle participe à l’amélioration continue, à la qualité des processus d’évaluation et à la crédibilité des dispositifs de certification. Au-delà de sa mission d’évaluation, l’expert tiers indépendant constitue aujourd’hui un véritable partenaire stratégique des organisations. En favorisant des décisions objectives, une meilleure maîtrise des risques et une culture de la qualité, il contribue durablement à la performance, à la compétitivité et à la création de valeur. À l’heure où la confiance devient un actif stratégique des organisations, l’expertise indépendante s’affirme non seulement comme une garantie d’impartialité, mais aussi comme un facteur déterminant de bonne gouvernance, de performance durable et de création de valeur pour l'ensemble de la chaîne des parties prenantes. En Tunisie, l’intégration progressive de ces missions au cœur de la gouvernance de nos organismes constitue désormais un enjeu stratégique. Leur appropriation par les responsables de gestion et leur intégration dans les processus académiques de formation permettront non seulement de renforcer notre efficacité et notre productivité, mais également de consolider durablement notre savoir-faire administratif et technique. Cette évolution représente ainsi un véritable levier de création de valeur, de modernisation et de performance institutionnelle. * Conseillers du Doyen de l’Ordre des Ingénieurs Tunisiens (OIT). L’IA et l’ingénieur de demain Références bibliographiques : ISO/IEC 17024:2012 – Conformity Assessment – General Requirements for Bodies Operating Certification of Persons. ISO 9001:2015 – Quality Management Systems – Requirements. ISO 31000:2018 – Risk Management – Guidelines. ISO/IEC 17020:2012 – Conformity Assessment – Requirements for the Operation of Various Types of Bodies Performing Inspection. Règlement (UE) n° 910/2014 – relatif à l'identification électronique et aux services de confiance. Publications de l'OCDE sur la gouvernance publique, l'intégrité et la confiance. Publications de la Banque mondiale relatives à la résolution des différends commerciaux et à la gouvernance. L’article Expertise indépendante et positionnement international de la Tunisie est apparu en premier sur Kapitalis .

items.title: Gunmen abduct two farmers in Ondo, demand N10m ransom
items.description: …Police launch rescue operation By Dayo Johnson, Akure Suspected gunmen have abducted two farmers in Ifira, an agrarian community in Akoko South-East Local Government Area of Ondo State. Sources said the attackers ambushed the victims on their way to the farm and whisked them into a nearby forest. A family member of one of the victims said the incident had been reported to the police and the Ondo State Security Network Agency, codenamed Amotekun. According to the source, the kidnappers have established contact with the families and are demanding a ransom of N10 million for the victims’ release. One of the relatives pleaded for urgent intervention. “We’ve reported the incident to the police and the Amotekun Corps in our area. We’re begging the security agencies and the government to help us rescue our brothers unharmed,” the family member said. Reacting to the incident, the spokesperson for the Ondo State Police Command, DSP Jimoh Abayomi, confirmed that a report of missing persons had been received at the Isua Akoko Divisional Headquarters. He said a joint security team comprising personnel of the Nigeria Police Force, the Nigerian Army, the Amotekun Corps, local hunters, Agbekoya operatives and community volunteers had been deployed to the Oke-Ifira/Idogun forest axis to search for the victims. “Search and rescue operations are currently ongoing, with all available operational and intelligence resources being deployed to locate the victims and bring the perpetrators to justice,” Abayomi said. The police spokesperson, however, noted that the command was not aware of any ransom demand in connection with the incident. “The Command wishes to categorically state that it is not aware of any ransom demand in connection with this incident,” he added. Abayomi appealed to members of the public with credible information to assist the ongoing rescue efforts, assuring that all information received would be treated confidentially. He reaffirmed the command’s commitment to ensuring the safe rescue of the victims and the arrest of those responsible, while urging residents to remain calm, vigilant and continue supporting the police with timely and credible information. The post Gunmen abduct two farmers in Ondo, demand N10m ransom appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Christian-Christian ticket will win Nigeria’s presidency one day — Gov Sule Abdullahi
items.description: Governor Abdullahi Sule of Nasarawa State has expressed confidence that Nigeria will eventually elect a president and vice-president on a Christian-Christian ticket, insisting that the country’s Constitution does not require religious balancing in political appointments or electoral tickets. Sule stated this on Tuesday during an appearance on Channels Television’s Politics Today while responding to criticism by the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) vice-presidential candidate, Rabiu Kwankwaso, over issues of political representation. The governor dismissed the criticism as a normal feature of opposition politics. “I think it is normal for Senator Kwankwaso, whom I respect a lot, to say that because he’s opposition. He cannot say anything good. It is expected of him to say that,” he said. Sule argued that the 1999 Constitution focuses on inclusiveness and federal character rather than religion. According to him, Sections 14(3) and 15 of the Constitution address equitable representation and ethnicity, but make no provision for religious balancing. “There is no place where it talks about religion. Section 14 didn’t talk about religion. Section 15 did not talk about religion,” he said. The governor cited the victories of President Bola Tinubu and Vice-President Kashim Shettima in 2023, as well as the late MKO Abiola and Babagana Kingibe’s presidential ticket in 1993, as examples of successful Muslim-Muslim tickets. “You can see both elections have been clear that the Muslim-Muslim ticket has won. I pray that one day Nigeria will have a Christian-Christian ticket, and it will win; that’s what I pray to see,” Sule said. He, however, stressed that his decision to retain Emmanuel Akabe, a Christian, as deputy governor was based on competence rather than religion. “I have always had a Christian as a deputy, Emmanuel Akabe. We thought it was important because Emmanuel Akabe is competent and our state is different from Nigeria,” he said. Sule added that political realities differ across states, noting that states such as Enugu and Bayelsa already operate Christian-Christian leadership without controversy. The post Christian-Christian ticket will win Nigeria’s presidency one day — Gov Sule Abdullahi appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: MoMo and GhIPSS champion national development by digitising informal transport payments
items.description: MobileMoney Fintech LTD (MoMo) has launched the Ride with MoMo campaign to support national development by helping commercial drivers transition from cash-based transactions to safer, traceable and more formal digital payment practices. Launched in partnership with Ghana Interbank Payment and Settlement Systems (GhIPSS) through the GhQR platform, the campaign began in Accra and continued with a roadshow in Ho, the Volta Regional capital. It brought together ride-hailing drivers, transport unions, the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Authority, the Motor Traffic and Transport Department, the National Communications Authority, financial institutions and other stakeholders to promote the MoMo Merchant Wallet and GhQR as practical tools for bringing transport payments into the formal digital economy. Speaking at the launch in Accra, Chief Executive Officer of MobileMoney Fintech LTD, Shaibu Haruna, said the campaign was designed to solve real challenges drivers face in handling cash while helping them build more structured and better-documented businesses. “Ride with MoMo is more than convenience. It is about helping drivers separate personal and business income, keep proper records, and build a transaction history that can support future access to financial services,” Mr Haruna said. He said the campaign will bring ride-hailing, haulage and Pragya drivers into the MoMo Merchant network, giving them a formal business payment channel through which passengers can pay seamlessly by scanning a GhQR code using the MoMo App or any application that supports GhQR. “When passengers pay by scanning a QR code, drivers receive the exact fare instantly. It reduces cash risks, removes the challenge of change and makes daily transactions simpler for both drivers and passengers,” he added. Chief Business Officer of GhIPSS, Akosua Blay, said the initiative reflects the value of collaboration in expanding Ghana’s shared digital payment infrastructure to everyday businesses. “GhQR gives drivers one simple code through which they can receive payments from bank accounts, mobile money wallets and fintech wallets. This is how we make digital payments practical, inclusive and accessible for small businesses across the country,” Madam Blay said. She added that the partnership with MobileMoney Fintech LTD will help more informal businesses enter formal payment channels and support Ghana’s transition to a more inclusive digital economy. A representative of ride-hailing drivers in Accra welcomed the initiative, noting that digital payments would make their work safer and more efficient. “Receiving fares directly into a merchant wallet helps us reduce cash handling, avoid disputes over change and keep better records of our daily sales,” he said. In Ho, where the campaign is targeting Pragya riders and other commercial transport operators, a representative of the riders said the solution would help them serve customers who increasingly prefer digital payments. “Many passengers do not always carry cash. With GhQR, they can pay easily, and we can also keep better track of our business,” he said. The Ride with MoMo campaign forms part of MobileMoney Fintech LTD’s broader contribution to national development by driving financial inclusion, supporting safer transport payments and helping commercial drivers move from informal cash-based transactions to more structured digital business practices.

items.title: Telecel Ghana announces 2026 FESSP cohort to help close STEM’s gender gap
items.description: Telecel Ghana has unveiled the ten outstanding final-year female students selected for the 2026 cohort of its Female Engineering Students Scholarship Programme (FESSP), an early career development programme aimed at increasing female representation in the Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) field. The female scholars, drawn from the Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology (KNUST), the University of Ghana (UG), the University of Cape Coast (UCC) and the University of Mines and Technology (UMaT), were selected through a competitive process that assessed academic excellence, leadership potential and industry readiness. Each scholar receives financial support towards their final-year tuition, a new laptop, data bundle, professional mentorship, internship and national service opportunities. Outstanding participants are also considered for Telecel Ghana’s NextGen Graduate Programme and future employment opportunities. Speaking at the cohort announcement ceremony, Telecel Ghana’s Human Resources Director, Rachael Appenteng, described the FESSP as the telco’s flagship talent development programme that has created opportunities for young women in engineering and technology, equipping them to lead innovation and strengthen Ghana’s technology ecosystem. “Our mission is to create a culture of opportunity and excellence. FESSP reflects our belief that talent, when nurtured, transforms organizations and societies alike. We are proud to see these scholars embody resilience, innovation, and leadership” Ms. Appenteng said. Launched in 2011, FESSP has grown into one of Ghana’s longest-running talent development initiatives dedicated to developing female STEM talent. The programme has supported more than 100 young women, many of whom now hold successful careers within Telecel Ghana and across the engineering and technology sectors. Addressing the scholars, Telecel Ghana’s Chief Executive Officer, Ing. Patricia Obo-Nai encouraged them to approach the opportunity with curiosity, confidence and a willingness to learn from every experience. She said; “FESSP is more than a scholarship, it is an investment in Ghana’s future. By empowering young women in STEM, we are building the innovators and leaders who will drive our country’s progress.” Alumna Clara Acquah, who joined in 2019, encouraged scholars to embrace engineering’s versatility: “Engineering equips you with transferable skills. Use it as a springboard and be creative, be innovative, and keep learning.” Another alumna and a Telecel’s NextGen Graduate Executive, Sena Foli-Mensah, reflected on her own experience. “FESSP became a real door of opportunity for me. Be open-minded, stay curious, build relationships and never be afraid to ask questions. “At Telecel, you will be challenged, trusted with responsibility and expected to grow. Rise to those opportunities and believe in your abilities.” Parents also praised the programme’s impact, with Anthony, whose daughter is part of this year’s cohort, saying: “We are grateful for the opportunity. FESSP has strengthened our faith in STEM and the future of our children. “As parents, we will continue supporting our daughters to embrace innovation, critical thinking and excellence, and we appreciate Telecel’s commitment to their development.” New scholar, Nana Ama Ampofowaa Antwi, said, “Securing a place in this year’s FESSP was quite competitive, so I’m grateful for the opportunity to be part of it. I look forward to making the most of this experience and continuing to grow and build my skills in STEM.” With each cohort, Telecel’s FESSP strengthens Ghana’s technology ecosystem by preparing engineers and scientists who will drive innovation, expand opportunities, and inspire others to pursue careers in STEM. Telecel Ghana remains committed to scaling this impact, ensuring that more young women step confidently into the future of science and engineering.

'He is a war criminal': Mamdani says NYC cannot arrest Netanyahu on ICC warrant, urges US federal officials to step in
items.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani said on Tuesday his office does not have the authority to carry out the International Criminal Court’s (ICC) arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, and called on US federal officials to step in. “Benjamin Netanyahu is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people,” Mamdani said in a brief video message posted to social media. “My administration has reviewed every avenue available under applicable law to determine whether New York City could execute the ICC’s arrest warrant if Benjamin Netanyahu came here,” he said. “It is clear that we do not have the independent legal authority to enforce this warrant. The federal government, however, does, and I call on them to join the ICC and execute this warrant.” The Hague-based International Criminal Court said in 2024 that it had reasonable grounds to believe Netanyahu was responsible for alleged war crimes and crimes against humanity related to Israel’s offensive in Gaza following the October 7, 2023 attack by Hamas. US President Donald Trump on Monday vowed the Israeli Prime Minister would not face arrest if he attended September’s United Nations General Assembly summit in New York. In the video, Mamdani said “anyone — with their eyes, with their heart, with their conscience — should recognise the devastation he has wrought and understand that he belongs before a court of law. “As I’ve said, I agree with the ICC that Benjamin Netanyahu should be arrested and tried for his crimes, as I do for anyone else charged by the ICC,” he added. Netanyahu’s office has dismissed Mamdani’s stance and said the ICC is a kangaroo court.

items.title: Dollar to Naira exchange rate today, July 22, 2026
items.description: The Nigerian naira traded at approximately ₦1,381.68 against the United States dollar at the Nigerian Foreign Exchange Market (NFEM) on Wednesday, July 22, 2026, according to official market data. The official exchange rate showed the naira exchanging at ₦1,381.68/$1 in the NFEM, reflecting the prevailing market rate at the start of trading on Wednesday. In the parallel market, commonly known as the black market, the dollar traded at around ₦1,408 per dollar for buying and ₦1,413 per dollar for selling, although rates may vary slightly depending on location, transaction size and dealer. Based on the prevailing rates, $100 would exchange for about ₦138,168 at the official market, while the same amount could fetch about ₦141,300 at the parallel market selling rate. The difference between the official and parallel market rates continues to reflect demand and supply dynamics in Nigeria’s foreign exchange market. The Central Bank of Nigeria has maintained reforms aimed at improving liquidity and narrowing the gap between the two exchange windows, although the street market continues to trade at a premium over the official rate. Exchange rates in the parallel market are not fixed and may change several times during the day based on market conditions, dealer quotations and the volume of transactions. Likewise, the official NFEM rate may fluctuate as trading progresses. The post Dollar to Naira exchange rate today, July 22, 2026 appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: OPay Leads Nigeria’s Fight Against Digital Fraud
items.description: By:Cynthia Alo How Smart Technology Is Protecting Trust in Africa’s Largest Digital Economy Company Says Trust, Not Just Speed, Will Define The Next Phase Of Financial Services In Africa’s Largest Market LAGOS – As Nigeria completes one of the fastest transitions from cash to digital payments on the continent, a new battlefront has opened in the country’s financial sector: the fight for trust. At a media parley held in Lagos on Thursday, July 16, 2026, leading fintech OPay said the defining competition among financial platforms is no longer who processes the highest volume of transactions, but who can guarantee that every single transaction is safe. The event, attended by media executives, technology analysts and industry stakeholders, focused on how artificial intelligence, behavioural analytics, facial verification and real-time monitoring are being deployed to protect millions of Nigerians from increasingly sophisticated fraud. The Trust Deficit In A Cashless Economy Nigeria has emerged as one of Africa’s fastest-growing digital payment markets. With mobile wallets replacing cash in markets, buses, and online stores, and with SMEs now depending on instant transfers to run daily operations, digital finance has become the backbone of the economy. But that growth has come with new risks. From identity theft and SIM-swap fraud to account takeovers and sophisticated scam transfers, criminals are evolving as fast as the technology meant to stop them. Consumers no longer judge financial platforms only by speed or convenience,” Elizabeth Wang, Chief Commercial Officer, OPay told journalists. “They also want to know one thing: ‘Will my money remain safe?’ The answer increasingly depends on the technology behind the app.” Wang said OPay’s long-term mission remains “making financial services more inclusive through technology,” with an aspiration to become the most respected and trusted financial technology company that creates social value. “When people trust digital financial services, they use them more confidently. That trust becomes the foundation for economic growth, for SMEs, for families, and for the country at large,” she added. From Customer Frustration To Engineering Solutions Taking journalists behind the scenes, Dotun Adekunle, Chief Operating Officer and Chief Technology Officer, OPay, explained that the company’s product roadmap starts with customer pain. Every feature in this story began as a customer’s frustration. Where the market said, ‘that’s just how payments work here,’ we treated it as an engineering problem waiting to be solved,” Adekunle said. He recalled that just a few years ago, many Nigerians still preferred cash because digital transfers could take hours to reflect, or in some cases disappear temporarily before being reversed. To solve this, OPay invested in redundant payment infrastructure and automatic failover systems. The system is designed to instantly reroute transactions whenever one pathway experiences difficulty. The result, according to the company, is a platform where 99.9 per cent of transactions are completed successfully. The average processing time now stands at less than 1 second. “Speed is not just about convenience,” Adekunle noted. “When a buyer and seller both get instant confirmation, anxiety drops. That is also a security feature because it reduces the window for doubt, disputes and social engineering.” Multiple Layers Of Protection: From Passwords To Faces The company said it has moved away from a one-size-fits-all approach to security. Instead, it runs a risk-based model where additional checks are triggered only when unusual activity is detected. For example, facial verification is automatically prompted for high-value transactions, payments from new devices, logins from unfamiliar locations, or transactions attempted at odd hours. “Your face is the final lock on risky transactions, invisible when things are normal, immovable when they are not,” Adekunle explained. This approach, he said, balances security with user experience. Customers are not interrupted at every step, but the system tightens automatically when the risk profile changes. Preventing Fraud before Money Leaves The Account A major theme of the parley was the shift from reactive to preventive security. Rather than waiting to reverse fraud after it happens, OPay said it is investing in tools that stop suspicious activity in real time. The company unveiled several customer-facing features: 1. Emergency Lock : Allows users to instantly freeze their accounts if a phone is lost, stolen, or if they suspect unauthorized access. 2. Night Guard : Requires facial verification for high-value transactions initiated late at night, a period often targeted by fraudsters. 3. Large Transaction Shield : Lets customers set personal limits. Any transaction above the limit triggers extra verification. 4. Anti-Scam Shield : Uses machine learning and behavioural analytics to detect suspicious transfer patterns and block them before funds exit the network. Modern fraud prevention should happen before money leaves an account,” Adekunle said. “AI and behavioural analysis allow us to see patterns humans cannot see, and to act in milliseconds.” The Invisible Workforce Behind Every Payment The executives emphasized that customers rarely see the work that goes into keeping digital payments safe. Behind every tap are hundreds of engineers, 24/7 system monitoring, advanced risk scoring, and continuous model training. Adekunle said most technical issues are identified and resolved before users ever notice them. The company also operates a “zero scheduled downtime” policy, meaning upgrades and maintenance are deployed without interrupting transactions. “We engineer for your safety, your reliability, and your peace of mind,” he stated. Chukwudinma Okafor, Chief Compliance Officer, also spoke on the company’s commitment to regulatory alignment, customer education, and collaboration with law enforcement and industry bodies to tackle financial crime. Beyond Payments: Building Social Value Wang noted that OPay’s investment in trust extends beyond technology. The company has also committed resources to financial literacy, education, innovation hubs, and community development programs. The cashless economy cannot succeed without security, and security cannot succeed without inclusion,” she said. “Our goal is to ensure that as more Nigerians come online financially, they come into a system that protects them.” What This Means For Nigeria’s Digital Future Industry analysts at the event agreed that Nigeria’s digital economy will continue to expand rapidly, but sustained growth will depend on confidence. Every successful transaction strengthens public trust. Every fraud attempt prevented reinforces that trust. And every innovation that makes financial services safer encourages more Nigerians, especially those previously excluded, to embrace digital payments. The consensus from the OPay parley was clear: the next phase of fintech in Nigeria will be defined not just by faster payments or bigger wallets, but by how effectively technology protects people. As fraud becomes more sophisticated, the industry’s response must become even smarter. The race, OPay executives said, is no longer simply about stopping criminals. It is about engineering trust into every transaction. _Pictured L-R: Dotun Adekunle, Chief Operating Officer and Chief Technology Officer, OPay; Chukwudinma Okafor, Chief Compliance Officer, OPay; Joshua Folayemi, PR Lead, OPay; Elizabeth Wang, Chief Commercial Officer, OPay; and Bayode Bajeh, Customer Service Manager, OPay, at the OPay Media Parley held in Lagos._ The post OPay Leads Nigeria’s Fight Against Digital Fraud appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Ojo council accused of demolishing property despite court order
items.description: By Innocent Anaba A property dispute involving Ojo Local Government Area, Lagos State, has taken a fresh turn following allegations that officials of the council demolished a property despite a subsisting judgment of a Lagos State High Court declaring the owner the lawful occupier and restraining further trespass. The property owner, Ibrahim Abubakar, alleged that about 1p.m., July 15, 2026, a top official of the council, accompanied by police officers and suspected thugs, led the demolition of his property at Alaba International Market without any court order. According to the claimant, the demolition was carried out despite a judgment delivered by Justice S. Sonaike of the Lagos State High Court on February 6, 2025, in Suit No. BD/1020LMW/2015, which affirmed his ownership of the disputed land. In the judgment, the court declared that Abubakar was entitled to the statutory right of occupancy over the parcel of land measuring approximately 1,679.177 square metres, situated behind Alaba Market, Oniba Road, Sabo Oniba Town, Ojo Area of Lagos State, and covered by Certificate of Occupancy No. 80 at Page 80 in Volume 2007 AC. The court ordered that the claimant should repossess the portion of the land trespassed upon by the defendants and granted a perpetual injunction restraining Ojo council, its agents, servants and assigns from trespassing on the property. Justice Sonaike also awarded the claimant N1 million in damages after holding that he had established exclusive possession of the property before the defendants’ unjustifiable intrusion, entry and destruction. Although the claimant had sought N20 million as damages, the court awarded N1 million while granting the substantive declaratory and injunctive reliefs. The suit was instituted on June 26, 2015, with Abubakar seeking declarations of title, repossession of the land, a perpetual injunction and damages for alleged trespass. Reacting to the latest development, the claimant alleged that although the defendants filed a notice of appeal challenging the judgment, they had taken no further steps to prosecute the appeal. He stated that the defendants also filed an application for stay of execution, which remains pending before the court. According to the claimant, rather than pursue the appeal or await the court’s determination of the pending application for stay of execution, officials of the local government allegedly resorted to self-help. “Our client had already obtained a valid judgment in respect of the property. Although the defendants filed a notice of appeal, they have not taken any further steps to prosecute it. They also filed an application for stay of execution, which is still pending before the court. “Instead of allowing the court to determine the pending processes, they went onto the land and demolished our client’s property.” The claimant maintained that the demolition was carried out in defiance of the subsisting judgment, which not only affirmed his title to the land but also restrained the defendants from further acts of trespass. At press time, thw Ojo council and the Nigeria Police Force had not responded to the allegation. The post Ojo council accused of demolishing property despite court order appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: GH¢4.2m deducted from National Service Personnel allowances for now-suspended training programme
items.description: About GH¢4.2 million has been deducted from the allowances of national service personnel deployed to government institutions nationwide to fund a capacity-building programme that has since been suspended amid concerns from beneficiaries. Director-General of the National Service Authority, Moses Doknach Kpeungu, disclosed that 99,508 national service personnel had been deployed across the country, including nurses. Of this number, nearly 29,000 were deployed to private sector institutions, meaning approximately 70,508 personnel were serving in government institutions and were therefore affected by the deduction Each affected national service person had GH¢60 deducted from their May allowance. The National Service Personnel Association (NASPA), explained that total amount represents a GH¢15 monthly deduction over four months to fund the capacity-building programme. JoyNews Research calculations based on the estimated 70,508 personnel serving in government institutions, the deduction amounts to approximately GH¢4.2 million. The association, however, maintains that the initiative was designed to bridge the gap between graduates and employment by equipping young people with additional skills beyond their academic qualifications The training programme comprised 5 courses and was scheduled to be delivered nationwide by Zeus Atlas Limited In an interview, NASPA President Abdul Wahab Bala Mohammed, explained the initiative was first piloted in the Greater Accra Region in 2024. “The response from the piloting stage was very massive and encouraging. So, we decided to upscale it nationwide. Hence, we took it to council, got the approval, went ahead to contract Zeus Atlas Limited to carry out the training” The programme has since been suspended following a directive from the Ministry of Youth Development and Empowerment, after concerns were raised over the deductions and the implementation of the initiative Some national service personnel have questioned the need for the programme, arguing that they have not participated in any training sessions and therefore should not have been subjected to the deduction. An affected National Service Person said “We do not agree to the fact that we will not receive a refund. First of all, we are not beneficiaries. You don’t even inform us about the capacity building. Some of us are NSS people, but we are not children. Because of this delay, I had to use my profit and some of my capital to be coming to work, and to feed myself at work.” The development, however, raises a broader question about representation and consultation: how did a programme intended to benefit national service personnel, and backed by an association representing them, become the subject of such strong resistance from some of the very people it was designed to serve?

items.title: 20 Nigerians die in Camerounian boat mishap claims
items.description: By Umar Yusuf Yola—No fewer than 20 persons mainly Nigerians have been confirmed killed following a boat mishap within the Cameroun territorial waters. The ill fated boat accident which took off from Garwa was heading to Yola in Adamawa State, when the incident happened. It was gathered that the boat with over 50 passengers was over loaded with iron rods and other building materials with a destination at Dasin in Fufore Local Government Area of Adamawa State. Locals within the Nigerian territorial waters confirmed that the boat took off late Monday night despite repeated advice for the operators to shift the journey till the following morning, but the advice was ignored. According to the locals, the ill fated boat ran into a storm late mid night and capsized killing more than 30 persons on board. So far, 18 victims are confirmed to have been recovered and laid to rest in Wuro Boki in Fufore, while the search for more victims is still on going. Locals confirmed that a search party of divers from the two countries have been in the waters for more victims even as some of the survivors are spread in neighbouring hospitals in Nigeria and Cameroun border villages. The Nigeria Police Marine unit in Adamawa when reached for comment claimed the incident happened outside the shores of the country. Nigeria and Cameroun trade relationship has continued to wax stronger especially within the maritime territorial border towns and villages. The post 20 Nigerians die in Camerounian boat mishap claims appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Xénophobie en Afrique du Sud : « Ils m’ont frappé avec une hache, j’ai été blessé », témoigne Souleymane Barry, de retour en Guinée
items.description: Rentré en Guinée lundi dernier à 17 heures après avoir fui les violences visant les étrangers en Afrique du Sud, Souleymane Barry a livré à Mediaguinee un témoignage bouleversant. Rencontré ce mardi à Dar-Es-Salam, le visage marqué par les épreuves qu’il dit avoir traversées, ce Guinéen peine encore à réaliser qu’il est de retour dans son pays. Il affirme avoir fui une vague de violences ciblant les ressortissants étrangers, raconte le rôle joué par l’ambassade de Guinée pour faciliter son retour et lance un appel en faveur des Guinéens encore bloqués sur place. « Les Sud-Africains ne veulent plus voir les étrangers noirs chez eux. Ils sont entrés dans nos boutiques, ils ont attaqué des gens dans les rues. Moi, je suis resté caché dans ma boutique, à l’étage, pendant plus d’une semaine. Dès qu’on sortait, les voisins criaient : “Il y a des étrangers ici !” On ne pouvait plus circuler », raconte-t-il. Selon lui, plusieurs de ses proches ont été victimes d’agressions. « J’ai des oncles et des amis là-bas. Certains ont été frappés, d’autres blessés à la tête. Moi-même, ils m’ont frappé avec une hache. J’ai été blessé », témoigne-t-il. Face à cette situation, Souleymane Barry explique avoir sollicité l’ambassade de Guinée. « Nous avons appelé l’ambassade au secours. Elle est venue nous récupérer et nous a conduits à Pretoria. J’y suis resté avec des amis avant de réunir l’argent nécessaire pour acheter mon billet de retour en Guinée », explique-t-il. Pour Souleymane Barry, ces violences trouvent leur origine dans un profond ressentiment envers les travailleurs étrangers. « Ils disent que les étrangers prennent leur travail. Pourtant, dans beaucoup de commerces, ce sont des étrangers qui travaillent parce qu’ils sont plus réguliers. Beaucoup de Sud-Africains quittent leur poste pour aller boire de l’alcool et reviennent incapables de travailler. Le week-end, certains disparaissent jusqu’au lundi. C’est pour cela que les commerçants indiens, pakistanais ou bangladais préfèrent employer des étrangers », affirme-t-il. Il estime que cette réalité alimente la colère d’une partie de la population locale. « Ils nous ont dit de quitter leur pays. C’est ça le problème aujourd’hui », ajoute-t-il. « Les attaques ont commencé après le 30 » Souleymane Barry affirme que les violences auraient été précédées d’un appel demandant aux étrangers de quitter le pays avant une date précise. « Il y avait un programme annonçant que, le 30, tous les étrangers devaient partir. Beaucoup n’y ont pas cru. Le 30, les policiers sont venus pour sécuriser la situation. Mais le 2, les attaques ont commencé. Ils entraient dans les boutiques, frappaient les étrangers avec des haches et certains étaient tués », raconte-t-il. Il évoque également des scènes particulièrement violentes. « Près de chez nous, des Malawiens et des Zimbabwéens ont été attaqués. Presque 17 personnes auraient été tuées en une seule nuit. Après cela, beaucoup ont commencé à fuir », affirme-t-il. Le Guinéen dit avoir décidé de quitter définitivement le pays après avoir assisté à cette montée de violence. « Je ne pouvais plus rester. Voir des gens sortir des fusils et tuer des personnes, c’était devenu insupportable. Vendredi dernier encore, ils ont brûlé un Congolais qui faisait de la boxe. Ils ont incendié sa maison et il est mort à l’intérieur », confie-t-il. Interrogé sur le rôle joué par la représentation diplomatique guinéenne, Souleymane Barry salue son intervention. « L’ambassade de Guinée a fait de son mieux. Aller de Pretoria jusqu’à Maritzburg pour venir nous chercher demande des moyens. Ils ont pris leurs véhicules, acheté du carburant et sont venus nous évacuer. Ensuite, chacun est parti là où il pouvait être hébergé. Moi, une semaine plus tard, j’ai acheté mon billet pour rentrer », souligne-t-il. Toutefois, il précise que plusieurs compatriotes demeurent toujours en Afrique du Sud, faute de moyens financiers. « Beaucoup de Guinéens sont encore là-bas. Ils n’ont pas l’argent pour acheter leur billet d’avion », déplore-t-il. Avant de conclure, Souleymane Barry lance un appel aux autorités guinéennes ainsi qu’aux personnes de bonne volonté afin de venir en aide aux compatriotes encore bloqués en Afrique du Sud. « Les problèmes concernent tous les étrangers, pas seulement les Guinéens. Aujourd’hui, nous demandons aux autorités et aux personnes de bonne volonté d’aider nos compatriotes à acheter leurs billets pour rentrer au pays. Ils n’ont pas les moyens de le faire. » Christine Finda Kamano & Robert Kourouma L’article Xénophobie en Afrique du Sud : « Ils m’ont frappé avec une hache, j’ai été blessé », témoigne Souleymane Barry, de retour en Guinée est apparu en premier sur Mediaguinee.com .

items.title: Telecel Ghana employees bridge ICT education gap in Asikuma
items.description: Students of Asikuma Presby Primary and adjoining schools in the Asuogyaman District of the Eastern Region now have a modern space to learn digital skills for their Information and Computer Technology (ICT) education. Under the Employee Volunteerism Programme (EVP), Telecel Ghana Foundation has handed over a refurbished and equipped 20-unit ICT laboratory to the Asikuma community, amid excitement and cultural display. The project, led by the Legal, External Affairs and Finance teams, was selected under the annual Employee Volunteerism Programme (Red Hearts), which empowers staff to pitch, co-fund and implement community development initiatives. Speaking at the commissioning ceremony, Director of External Affairs at Telecel Ghana, Komla Buami, urged students, teachers and the wider school community to embrace the opportunities the facility offers. “This ICT laboratory is a gateway to knowledge, innovation and opportunity. My three requests are simple: use it, protect it and grow with it. “Let students make full use of this facility, let it be carefully maintained for future generations, and let our teachers continue building their own digital skills so they can prepare every learner to succeed in today’s digital world.” Beyond the Telecel Ghana Foundation’s seed funding, employees raised additional funds through internal crowdfunding to renovate the facility and equip it with computers, furniture and internet connectivity. Head of Foundation, Sustainability and External Communications at Telecel Ghana, Rita Agyeiwaa Rockson, said the project embodies the spirit of the Foundation’s Red Hearts initiative by placing employees at the centre of positive community transformation. “Red Hearts is about empowering our people to become changemakers. This project shows what is possible when employees identify a community need, mobilise support and work together to deliver lasting impact. “We hope this laboratory inspires students to dream bigger, explore technology and unlock their full potential,” Ms Rockson said. Receiving the facility on behalf of the school, the headmistress, Madam Regina Ayikpa, said the computer lab will change the appalling way students learn ICT in the school through theory. “The lab will give them practical exposure to technology and help them in their digital learning. The students will be equipped to participate in the current digital economy, and they can take advantage of digital work opportunities in the future.” The Chief of Asikuma Traditional Area, Nana Abuko Kofi I, commended Telecel Ghana’s benevolent employees for the ICT lab that will improve digital education and promised the community will maintain the equipment. “I am so grateful because I know the relevance of computers and digital education and how this lab will improve student performance. We will protect the devices and ensure they are all maintained,” he said. “Telecel deserves an office in Asikuma, and I will switch to your network for this positive deed.” Speaking on behalf of her colleagues, Basic 6 pupil Jessica Avagah said the laboratory would help pupils understand ICT better through practice. “The new computer lab will make practical lessons easier and faster. We will learn how to type and get information online for our class topics. Thank you for the computers, as it will make our future brighter,” she said. This year, the Asikuma ICT Laboratory is the second project delivered under Telecel Ghana Foundation’s Red Hearts. In May, employees handed over a renovated, connected and fully equipped 30-seater ICT Lab to the Akim Achiase district. Red Hearts reflect Telecel Ghana Foundation’s commitment to improving access to quality digital education and healthcare delivery and encouraging employees to drive meaningful social impact in communities across Ghana.

items.title: Kanu’s lawyer threatens law suit over TV comment
items.description: By Steve Oko UMUAHIA — Lead counsel to the detained leader of the Indigenous People of Biafra, IPOB, Mazi Nnamdi Kanu, Mr Aloy Ejimakor, has threatened legal action over an alleged public call for the extrajudicial killing of his client. Ejimakor, in a statement yesterday, condemned what he described as a dangerous and unlawful remark allegedly made by Mr Chigozie Nnabugwu during a programme aired on News Central Television. According to the lawyer, Nnabugwu allegedly urged the Federal Government to kill Kanu so that “normalcy would return to the South-East.” He said the comment, which was reportedly aired on July 19 and later posted on News Central Television’s verified X handle, had since attracted widespread attention. Describing the statement as “reckless, irresponsible and unlawful,” Ejimakor argued that publicly advocating the killing of a citizen whose case is pending before a court of competent jurisdiction amounts to an attack on the rule of law and due process. He said the remark could endanger Kanu’s life, incite violence and undermine public peace. According to him, the alleged statement may amount to an offence under Section 24(1) of the Cybercrimes Act, which criminalises the transmission of electronic messages capable of threatening life or causing a breakdown of law and order. Ejimakor noted that the offence carries penalties, including a fine, imprisonment or both upon conviction. He further argued that the alleged comment could also attract liability under other laws relating to incitement and related offences. The lawyer also faulted News Central Television for airing the remark, stressing that media organisations have a responsibility to prevent the dissemination of content capable of promoting violence or encouraging unlawful acts. According to him, broadcasters that provide platforms for statements perceived as hate speech or calls for violence could face regulatory scrutiny for possible breaches of broadcasting standards. Ejimakor further contended that advocating the killing of an individual while judicial proceedings are ongoing could be seen as an attempt to interfere with the administration of justice. He disclosed that Kanu’s legal team was reviewing available legal options, including petitions to relevant regulatory and law enforcement agencies. The lawyer demanded an immediate retraction from the individual involved and a public apology from the television station, warning that legal proceedings could follow if the demands are not met. Kanu, leader of IPOB, is currently facing terrorism-related charges before a Federal High Court in Abuja. His trial has continued to attract national and international attention, with his legal team maintaining that his constitutional rights and due process must be respected. The post Kanu’s lawyer threatens law suit over TV comment appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: 9 weeks after IMF exit, gov’t is back for $1bn in loans – Oppong Nkrumah questions fiscal strategy
items.description: Ranking Member on Parliament’s Economy and Development Committee, Kojo Oppong Nkrumah, has questioned the government’s fiscal strategy. He argues that barely nine weeks after Ghana exited the International Monetary Fund (IMF) Extended Credit Facility (ECF) programme, the Mahama administration is back in Parliament seeking approval for almost $1 billion in fresh loans. According to the Ofoase-Ayirebi MP, the latest borrowing requests raise concerns about the government’s domestic revenue mobilisation efforts and the country’s long-term debt sustainability. Speaking during debate on a motion seeking parliamentary approval for a number of international financing agreements, Mr Oppong Nkrumah urged the House not to assess each loan in isolation but to consider the cumulative borrowing taking place within a short period after the IMF programme ended. Opening his remarks with the French expression, “Plus ça change, plus c’est la même chose” —meaning “the more things change, the more they remain the same” —he argued that despite government’s declaration that Ghana had successfully exited the IMF programme, the country’s dependence on external borrowing appears to be continuing. He recalled that on May 15, 2026, the government announced Ghana’s successful exit from the IMF ECF programme, describing it as a major milestone in the country’s economic recovery. However, he noted that just nine weeks later, Parliament is being asked to approve borrowing close to $1 billion. According to him, the package includes about $300 million for the education sector, $500 million for road infrastructure, roughly $22 million for the Ministry of Finance, in addition to net borrowing of about $180 million already captured in the 2026 Budget. Mr Oppong Nkrumah clarified that the Minority is not opposed to the projects being financed. Having served in both government and opposition, he acknowledged that development financing serves legitimate national purposes and stressed that his concern was not with the sectors earmarked to benefit from the loans. Instead, he argued that the key issue is why the government has returned to the debt market so soon after the IMF programme ended. The former Information Minister said government had assured Ghanaians during the 2026 Budget that it had a stronger fiscal strategy capable of removing taxes while increasing revenue to 18 per cent of GDP. He said he publicly supported that target at the time. However, he argued that government’s own fiscal reports show the target has not been achieved. According to him, one official report places the revenue-to-GDP ratio at 15.7 per cent, while another records 16 per cent, levels he said are no better than what the current administration inherited. “It is because the domestic resource mobilisation measures are not performing that nine weeks after the IMF left town, they have gone back to the debt markets and are asking Parliament to approve close to one billion dollars.” While supporting the loan agreements, Mr Oppong Nkrumah cautioned that excessive borrowing could undermine Ghana’s debt sustainability if domestic revenue performance does not improve. He also criticised what he described as government spending priorities, arguing that resources are increasingly being directed towards quasi-fiscal operations instead of critical development needs. Citing figures from the Bank of Ghana’s 2025 financial statements, he claimed that about GH¢16 billion had been spent on sterilisation operations, while approximately GH¢9.6 billion had been incurred through gold-related losses. He proposed that government urgently strengthen domestic revenue mobilisation and adopt a whole-of-government approach to expenditure management to reduce reliance on external borrowing. Mr Oppong Nkrumah concluded by reaffirming the Minority’s support for the loan agreements but insisted that government must address the underlying weaknesses in revenue generation and spending if Ghana is to avoid renewed debt pressures.
